Drew well, and would have put a Su-Chi on the board turn-2 if not for the Relic Barrier on Mana Vault. Had a Basalt Monolith, another Su-Chi and a Yawggy in-hand too. Tough loss, but an encouraging end to their first season.

Things. Revenge smackdown. The Things pulverize the Hot by using "Living Death"  to bring the usual trio onto the battlefield along with a Hidden Horror. 

Hot. Did their best to counteract some of the initial offense by the Things, nullifying an early Hidden Horror and adding 16-life to their names via two Ivory Tower. Wasn't enough though.

Things. ...phew. This was too close for comfort, but they pulled it out thanks to a Necromancy-into-Polar Kraken. They fended off a Boomerang and then blasted the Hot the next turn. Game over. Series over. 

Hot. Wow, closer than anyone would've thought. They were right in this one thanks to the same Control Magic duo that won Game 1. Ran out of tricks though and meet their seasons' end as a result.

Glop. Big win!!! Struggle a top dog thanks to Winter Orb. The orb gave the Artists enough time to put two Nought's in play thanks to a Mask. An early Balance was huge also to clear a path.They won despite having only 3 lands all game.

Potatoes. Win this game without Winter Orb in play. Sat on two Armageddon, a Wrath of God and others. Tough loss against a seemingly strong opponent.

Stormy. Get one back against the foe who took their lunch last playoffs. Rain heavy bolts left and right, including 9-damage in one turn. A Ball Lightning accounted for another six and we're just about there.

Doom. Looked extremely lethargic. They might be caught in the middle of fast and slow. Might need to take another look at their roster.

Gloves. So fun!! The synergy on this team was awesome. They played one creature all game - Shocker - and he recorded not one point of damage. Black Vise took the first load, then a second Underworld Dreams paired exceptionally well with a Winds of Change and Teferi's Puzzle Box to knock out the Artists. What a fun team.

Artists. Had a tough time getting going with all the hand shuffling. Tough opponent for them because their mana manipulation didn't help much.

Slivers. Three different dual lands gave way to 5 different Slivers. Once Crystalline hit the battlefield it was over for Darwin. The Hurricane they had in-hand became useless. Nice win by the Slivers to start the season.

Darwin. Start their 6th season in disjointed fashion against one of the original ten (O-10). Fallow Wurm showed a huge weakness - the high likelihood that you simply cannot summon the beast. What good is a 4/4 at 3MC if you can't put it in play? No good. Might need a Djinn substitute or two.

Doom. Claw back to erase an early deficit by throwing professional Big Boy FoN out onto the field. He does away with the meager Things defense of a STDevil, Shadow and Hidden Horror easily to reclaim victory.

Things. Were looking good until Darwin put a Erhnam Djinn onto the battlefield. It was about sustaining then, but they didn't draw any additional help.

Monsters. Incredible win. They were down 19 to 13 on turn-6. The Gloves use a Winds of Change to look for anything that might help them kill the Nought ready to deal 12-damage the next turn. They pull a Demonic Tutor, then use it to find a Jokulhaups. Eureka. The Monsters, however, swapped out five cards and drew an Island, another Nought, two Unstable Mutation and....a Counterspell. They had defense agains the oncoming board-clearer but wouldn't need it thanks to the two Mutation. 20 damage was done the following turn thanks also to a hidden Ornithopter.

Gloves. SMACK. Let's see if they can't pull themselves together after that mighty blow.

Things. Three mana was all it took to put a Hidden Horror, a STDevils and a Polar Kraken into play for the win. The Horror benefited from a Unstable Mutation to deal a few heavy blows early. Darwin tried to contain the Kraken with a FoN but it wasn't enough. Is anything?

Darwin. Tough loss when a Archers, Ants and a Force gets into play. That might be the only time in history it's happened. Also highly unusual that the Force lost in hand-to-hand combat.

Gloves. Looked REAL good in this game. Two early Black Vise ground the Monsters down, then a U-Dreams / Tefari's Puzzle Box closed them out. Six quick turns.

Monsters. Were stifled by mana issues with only one Island in play for most of the game - two Maze of Ith were also in play but they didn't help against a creature-less opponent.

Things. Phew, they got lucky here. On 4th-turn, they use two Dark Ritual to throw two Kraken and a Necrosavant into the graveyard then a Tombstone Stairwell. They then draw a Living Death three turns in a row without the mana to cast. The foible allowed Darwin to build up a nice lead with a Y-Ants, but thankfully drew one more Dark Ritual to bring the big boys into play for a big W.

Doom. Ugh, close. Pushed the Things down to 2-life, but have literally no answer for the dual-Kraken.

Gloves. Cruise to victory behind a U-Dreams with twelve traditional draw damage, then a 5-point Sudden Impact, then 3 more from Winds of Change. Easy win. Fun play was putting a Teferi's Puzzle Box out, followed by a Winds of Change. The Box was countered, but it meant nearly 10-damage in one turn. Nice.

Monsters. The perfect game to review with a fine-toothed comb given their primary struggles on O&D. Here were the wasted cards: Mana Vortex, Howling Mine, Reconstruction, then Relic Barrier and Meekstone. A lone Ornithopter led the attack.

Monsters. So the Mana Vortex can help them!!! Their strong creature defense players sat the bench in this one as Mana Vortex turned the tide against the Gloves. They forced an aggressive move in Mist putting two Howling Mine in play which led directly to a Mask-Nought combo. Two Unstable Mutation put our Noughty friend at a near 20/20. Outrageous. The Relic Barrier helped hasten things, too.

Gloves. The dual Mine move was made out of desperation knowing that a low-mana game likely hurt their chances at winning. The move backfired, but a risk well-taken nonetheless.

Things. Wild game. They use two Unstable Mutation on a STD early and only deal 4-damage before dying off. They lost a Hidden Horror and another STD, then used a Tombstone Stairwell to put more pressure on Darwin. Once killing off most of their defense, they subbed a Necrosavant for the lone remaining STD and took it home.

Doom. Missing something here. The game was up for grabs early - normally when Darwin is at its' best - but they couldn't capitalize. Unfortunately, their C-Kill cards like Winter Blast and Hurricane couldn't hurt the pesky might mites on the other end. Their creatures - Ants, Ranger - were also totally nullified by the first strike of the STDevil. That's the end of their season, too. Might need more defense in here - Walls?

Monsters. Whoooaaaaaa Monsters!! The long game favors then, and man did that hold true in the 2nd Round finale. They kept a meager Gloves offensive at bay with a few counters an held out for the Mask-Nought combo. It came on late thanks to a self-inflicted Arcane Denial and a few opposing Howling Mine. Two Nought made it out and destroyed two Shocker, two Spector, and Wisp. They win this game for a few reasons - 

(A) Early counters, one on a U-Dreams
(B) Draw advantage help
(C) Low mana cost spells to avoid bigger Spector
(D) Relic Barrier preventing a Puzzle Box from wreaking havoc - a quick count had them saving at least 10-damage which changes the game, and the playoff picture, huge!

Gloves. A fantastic first season in the books. This team is here to stay and is incredibly unpredictable. Very fun to play with. There's a few areas to look for improvement on both O&D, like C-Kill. They lose to a formidable foe who attacked with 30-damage from two Nought. Insane.

Things. Close call here, but a very positive sign for the offseason changes made, notably Unstable Mutation. They were eliminated in lsat post-season bc of their reliance on the "Certain Death" combo - which was nullified in this game. The game-MVP Shadow benefited from some C-Pump to withstand a Jokulhaups and win the game.

Gloves. Promising game despite the loss. One more turn likely nets them the victory, as they sat on a Vise-Mine combo in play. A Underworld Dreams was about to hit the battlefield with a hand full of H-Pump when Living Death was played - forcing the board clearing Jokulhaups. Close loss.
Show less

Doom. Drew exceptionally well to thwart 10s impending attack. Two defensive cards - Tranquility & Verdigris - we're critical in paving the way for a Force of Nature.

10. Had 11 mana on their second turn thanks to three Mana Vault's. Crazy. Had Planar Gate destroyed early which really hurt them. A late Yawggy was there but no match for the big boy in green. Royal Assassin was left stranded in hand too. Juuuuust about there!

Fault. Nice sharp win. Second turn Strip Mine and Dark Ritual-Hyppy out them out in front for good. To Megrim's showed up and a late Mind Twist TOTALLY dismantled Anti-Deck.

Anti. Tough loss with three lands destroyed in this one. Losing their hand to Mind Twist hurt them badly with Terri Clone's in hand and some funky stuff on the way. May need more counters to slow initial attack.

Potatoes. Win a battle of attrition against their rival. Used a Balance to knock out opposing lands and a Sengir Vampire. FAs like Land Tax and Cleansing really hamper NLFY. They could rely on cheaper creatures much more so than NLFY could, and win as a result.

NLFY. Amazing they were even able to dole out 11-damage in this one. They have real problems with the Potatoes new emphasis on land destruction, life pump and mana pump. Balance may have shifted toward the Potatoes in this match-up of Original 10 teams.

Things. Close call here, but a very positive sign for the offseason changes made, notably Unstable Mutation. They were eliminated in lsat post-season bc of their reliance on the "Certain Death" combo - which was nullified in this game. The game-MVP Shadow benefited from some C-Pump to withstand a Jokulhaups and win the game.

Gloves. Promising game despite the loss. One more turn likely nets them the victory, as they sat on a Vise-Mine combo in play. A Underworld Dreams was about to hit the battlefield with a hand full of H-Pump when Living Death was played - forcing the board clearing Jokulhaups. Close loss.

Stormy. Continue to beat up the Slivers following their 3-0 sweep in last seasons' playoffs. Newcomer Ball Lightning's dealt 12-damage in this one, and the rest came from direct damage. They look REAL feisty.

Slivers. Good mana and a 4 Muscle Slivers (last season MVP) looked positive from the start, but they haven't quite figured out how to deal with the constant firepower of Dark'n'Stormy.

Fault. Real ugly game. Only 5 lands played in 8-turns apiece. Fault put three in play after using a Strip Mine to get a dual-Shocker + Megrim play. A turn later they deal 30-damage for a W.

NLFY. Awful looking. Pulled one land all game. Then discarded 14-cards thanks to 2 Shocker and still only pulled 1 land. More balance in Game 2 hopefully.

Monsters. Hot damn, what a victory. This was one of the best games I've had to date. Somehow, the Monsters win this one. Here were the primary counterparts:

- Maze of Ith - incredible defense against two White Knight's for most of the game
- Relic Barrier - kept two Zuran Orb at bay preserving the chance at a W while also enabling two Noughts early on
- Unstable Mutation - the game MVP, all 4x of them came out to pump an Ornithopter and a Phantasmal Mount, the last one coming right in time to win the game.
- Mana Vortex - gutsy move, but it forced the Potatoes into uncomfortable territory by threatening two Factory and a Outpost. This is what led to a 8th-turn Armageddon with sacrificed lands that eventually backfired due to Counterspell. Big turning point all thanks to Vortex.
- Howling Mine - proving its' worth yet again by speeding up the game in their favor

They win in 15'ish turns (lost track) but showed some incredible grit to sneak a W against the #1-seed. Wow.

Potatoes. Well that was just painful. Their mid-game Orb-Geddon combo was thwarted and it changed the entire trajectory of the game. Fortunately, they were able to kill off two Nought and were lucky a third didn't show up.

Sliver. The Slivers are thirsty for revenge after being eliminated in 3-straight two years ago. Game 1 was ugly with six slivers hitting the battlefield in 5-turns; Heart, Winged, Muscle and Talon, Muscle and Winged. They had a Coat of Arms in-hand too.

Things. Totally unprepared for this one. They were mana-strapped and could muster only a STDevil and a Buried Alive.

Extinction. Outstanding win. Early Lions gets killed off, then a Angelic Renewal helped kill off a Balduvian Horde as they began the uphill climb from being down big early. Two Kudzu helped keep a Shivan off the board, then a Cataclysm put the nail in the coffin by killing off everyone but a Djinn and Ape.

DnS. Had a big lead early thanks to a Ball Lightning, a Fork-Incinerate and others but couldn't keep it.

NLFY. No contest, they whack the Fault upside the noggin with two Black Vise - one thanks to a Tutor. Didn't face much adversity at all given Fault's mana issues.

Fault. Easy to say, but one more mana changes this game. They sat on a bunch of 2-and-3 CC cards to destroy hand, lands and deal some damage. Cmon Fault!!!

Stormy. Not much of a contest. Slung a ton of DD at X as they stockpiled mana to then throw down two Fireball and a Balduvian Horde for the win.

X. A creature-heavy team summons only 3 creatures?!!?! Cmon X, say it ain't so..

Slivers. Faced little adversity once again to go up 2-0. This time they used a Gemstone Mine and a City of Brass to rattle off 8 sliver en route to victory. City of Brass was key once the Mine went down to keep the assault on.

Things. Landed the "Certain Death" combo in the opening hand but never amounted the mana to summon the full thing. Three mana had them strapped.

Potatoes. Destruction. Just hand it to the Monsters thanks to 3x Crusade, 4x Wolves, 2x Knight and 2x Archer. Crazy beatdown.

Monsters. Two Howling Mine backfired, they never drew their Nought despite 3x Mask and a Relic Barrier in hand. Reconstruction looks fruitless when no Nought make it out. Felt like, with another Maze of Ith and counters, you could remove Relic Barrier, Howling Mine and Meekstone in this crew.

Extinction. Great win, take advantage of mana-strapped Stormy by withstanding some meager DD defense and end the game with a 7/8 Lhurgoyf facing a barren battlefield (thanks to Cataclysm). Kudzu played a big hand in the victory by killing off a critical Mountain - leaving a Furnace of Rath and other spells stranded in hand. Ton of dual-lands out in this one - beautiful sight to see!

Stormy. Drawing four lands in 16-turns is a doozie and they couldn't hang on despite an early 20-11 lead. The one mana lost was critical.

Slivers. Damn, that's some retribution right there. Put them away in 6-turns to hit the SWEEP button. Gemstone Mine is a great card for this deck, should grab more. Final attack showcased 2-Winged, 1-Heart, 1-Talon, 1-Crystalline, 2-Metallic. 

Series averages victory in 6-turns for Slivers with an avg. 7-deploys vs. 3.67 for their opponent. Speed kills!

Things. Well, rough season. Underwhelming regular season put them in the Qualifier bracket only to face a growing nemesis in Round 1. No one has the offensive firepower of the Slivers, but the Things didn't show nearly enough defense or speed either. Will need some adjustments in the off-season.

Fault. Surgical victory. Put a Megrim in play early, then kept NLFYs mana in check while dismantling their hand via 3-hitter Mind Twist, two Hyppy and a Pox.

NLFY. Vulnerable to the enchantment on the board and the two black creatures.

Stormy. Win a weird game that probably should've gone to X. They went punch for punch with X and landed the final one via B-Horde and a Fork/Chain Lightning combo.

X. Blew it. They had two Djinn and a Mana Birds on the field, and opted not to play a Wrath of God to knock out the opposing Horde. They lost the next turn. Stupid.

NLFY. No contest thanks to mana struggles for the Fault. Destroyed all 3x of their lands and put two Knight, one Vampire and a Vise in play to even the series.

Fault. Stumbled out the gate despite having a bruising hand of 3x Megrim, some H-Kill and a few creatures. Can't do that against NLFY tho.

Monsters. Hot damn, the Monsters are in it!! They force a series clinching-Game 5 against the #1-seed by throwing everything but the kitchen sink to win Game 4. They used a Howling Mine to speed things up, a Meekstone, Relic Barrier, Counters, Invoke Prejudice and a Mana Vortex to slow things down. They lost two Nought thanks to a balance, then revived them a few turns later with a Drafna's Restoration - first time ever used. Only one Nought subsided, and it cruised on to victory with the Monsters of the Mist having to deal 54-total damage to win. What a furious stand.

Potatoes. Drew the cards they needed, but are being beaten by the counter strength of the mono-blue team. They lost countless lands thanks to Armageddon gone wrong - may have to avoid the strategy in future match-ups with this team. Starting to sweat as Game 5 lingers...

Potatoes. Advance! Clinch the series behind some nifty M-Kill thanks to Armageddon. it took down two crucial defensive posts in Maze of Ith. They were then unfettered and pumped up by their Crusade to throw punch after punch. Monsters amounted some D but it wasn't enough.

Monsters. Not a bad run! They underwent a serious transformation throughout the season and emerged a feisty underdog by advancing out of the Qualifier Tourney and into the Premier bracket to then push the #1-seeded Potatoes to a Game 5 finale. Nice! They have the makings of a bruising squad - we've finally found the right color for the Mask-Nought combo!!!! Woohoo! More help on the way, but they are good at slowing it down and building up the pressure. A little more speed and offense will help. Excited to see what they can do with a full season.

NFLY. Just deadly when they're this fast. They used 2nd-turn to cast a Sengir Vampire via 2 Swamp and a Sol Ring. The Sengir to work from there. A Demonic Hordes, also thanks to a Dark Ritual, made it out a few turns later. Fault had no C-Kill to defend themselves.

Fault. Lost a battle of M-Kill early, which set them back only slightly. The real challenge was in not keeping up with the speed of Dark Ritual and Sol Ring - once out, they couldn't keep their summoning power on-par and met their end. Interesting season for the Fault who seemed much less formidable this time around. Will be fun to dig into their needs this off-season.

X. WHOA BABY! The resuscitated Extinction Team is onto the next Round!! The big game-changer in this one was Kudzu - dispatching 3 of the opposing lands that kept Stormy from throwing a few haymakers (Furnace of Rath among them). They used a critical WoG to kill off a Horde, then smacked 10-damage two turns later with a pumped up Djinn, and then finally ended it all with a Ape and Lhurgoyf. This is a fun team.

Potatoes. Big, big win against one of the only remaining unbeaten teams. An Armageddon on the 5th turn completely derailed the Things. They were able to subside on a White Knight, a flying Aesthir and a few other mighty mites for the win.

Things. They were one mana away from launching the second half of their "Certain Death" combo - Spirit of the Night, two Necrosavant's and a Nether Shadow sat idly in the graveyard waiting for the call - when they lost their lands. Recovery was tough, and they fought with a Hidden Horror and STDevils as best they could.

NLFY. Solid win. Ride two Sinkhole's, a Rain of Tears and a Choking Sands to keep a strong Big Gloves team at bay. They dealt 10-damage on turn-8 thanks to two Black Knight's, a Royal, a Choking Sands penalty and the ensuing Black Vise. Really good first win of the season for the defending champ!

Gloves. This team is good. Still figuring out the right way to play them, but man are they capable of putting the heat on fast. If not for the loss of a key Mountain, they would have won this game. Four total lands lost hurt them, but Underworld Dreams in play is as ferocious as it gets in this league.

Anti. Good opponent for them, stealing and animating artifacts to gain an edge. They stole a Royal Assassin and pumped him with Unstable Mutation and then Stasis froze the board and did a bunch of damage via an untapped Mana Vault. Sat back until they animated a Helm of Obedience for the win.

Team 10. Tough loss, but he's a little more defense in this deck. They gather mana until they can go on the offensive which makes them too vulnerable. Need some D.

Slivers. A first-turn Taiga helped put two Muscle Sliver's and a Heart Sliver in play fueling all the attack for the winner. They had even more firepower in hand, but had to take out a timely Meekstone with a Disenchant. Good win.

Glop. Two mana capped them early.They deployed more cards, but couldn't get anything going - leaving a Serra Angel, Storm Seeker and a Disenchant.

Extinction. Whoa nelly, this Team is on a tear. They outdraw the Potatoes in Game 1 with 7 creatures to their opponents' 3. Subside on only 4 lands thanks to two Birds and a Sol Ring. An Erhnam played some major D while a Lhurgoyf got going. I love Lhurgoyf.

Potatoes. Drop Game 1 thanks to too much mana...and losing a Zuran Orb. It was there only chance to stall the game, but it was discarded thanks to an opposing Disenchant.

This is going to be a great series.

NLFY. Great win, they stuck it out, knocked out 6 Slivers thanks to a Hellfire and a N-Disk. The N-Disk was activated just before a Crystalline Sliver hit the board - huge. They were mana-full and won on the heels of a Demonic Hordes.

Sliver. Lost 8-creatures in this game and were stuck in the mana department for much of the game.

Potatoes. Clean up show here, building on a Crusade-pumped Knight and Archer by wiping Xs lands from the board via Armageddon. Good thing, too, as the next draw for X would've enabled a WoG to hit the board. Potatoes win one turn later.

X. Mana strapped in this game and summoned only a Disenchant (Crusade) and a Sol Ring. The final land would've enabled the WoG and two Djinn.

NLFY. Killer comeback, down 18 to 6 and climb their way out. A Cyclopean Tomb proved PIVOTAL in this game as it dismantled any mana flexibility the Slivers had turned a handful into Swamps. This was entirely why they won. Five other lands were killed off and a staunch defense kept the game in check; Icy-Royal, Quicksand, N-Disk. Gadzooks, what a win.

Slivers. Ergh, met one of their true adversaries in C-Tomb. Had utterly no answer for it and went down in fiery flames as a result. Lost 6 slivers in this game and had 8-lands destroyed or Swamped.

X. Win the longest game in League History; 32 turns! Insanity. They went punch for punch to take a 2 games to 1 lead over the #1-seed Potatoes. They played only minimal defense in this game somehow, and outlasted the Potatoes who pulled nearly every trick in their book. Once the game hit the late 20s in turns, two Lhurgoyf's made it out thanks to two Manabirds and it was all over. Had 13 of 15 lands destroyed in this game. Wild win.

Potatoes. Wow, spent most of the game on the defensive, using 3x Armageddon and a Balance to hold off their opponent. Incredible that they were beat. A mid-game (12th turn) Balance helped stave off their demise, and it granted them 19 more turns -- insane! In the end, X was able to go on the offensive in a mana-strapped game more often than the Potatoes.

One for the history books.

NLFY. All cylinders firing in this one - they had strong mana supply, incredible C-Kill in 2x N-Disk and a Hellfire, then saw 3x Sengir Vampire in addition to Demonic Hordes and Black Knight. Damn! And this doesn't take 4x M-Kill into effect. Just a well balanced attack against the Slivers to take Game 3.

Lining. Real tough loss, they had 4x Slivers on the board with a Coat of Arms when the N-Disk went off. They were at a bit of a stalemate - only 3x A-Kill in the deck so they just went for it. They would've died a slower death had they sat tight.

Potatoes. Great win, they needed this one. Sat on the heels of great mana supply following the game changing Balance. Best X to the punch with small attackers and closed out quickly.

Extinction. Had the momentum early until Balance shook the board. Couldn't recover after drawing land after land.

Sliver. Drew the perfect set of Sliver creatures in this one. They had a Heart, Winged and Muscle in-hand, then used a Worldly Tutor to pull a Crystalline Sliver and put it out on turn-3. Turn-6 the Slivers lost 2x lands, or actually 3x, but summon Heart Sliver to turn the table by dealing 6-damage. Two turns later, it was over. An early Disenchant destroyed a Sol Ring on the other side and completely derailed NLFY.

NLFY. Used a Demonic Tutor to pull a Sol Ring that was destroyed one turn later. Brutal. They couldn't summon much, notably the N-Disk in-hand which would've changed the entire game.

Potatoes. Huge win to take the series lead 3-2. A few crucial moves were made in this game 
- 2x Crusade pumped mighty mites up
- Field of Souls delivered waves of attackers to finish off X
- Armageddon eliminated any chance of X putting a Djinn and Lhurgoyf into play

Potatoes are now in the driver seat thanks to two solid wins. They looked like the #1-seed in this game.

X. Second tough loss in a row now. They lost 5x creatures in this game and were so close from putting heft into the battlefield before Armageddon came. They really could've used some C-Kill in this game to tip the scales a bit.

Sliver. WHOA nelly, here come the Slivers!!! First land (Forest) is destroyed, and they retaliated by soon killing the Black Vise on the board. They then go on a roll of Plateau, Gemstone Mine, Underground River, Island, Undiscovered Paradise afterward to unleash a Crystalline, Winged, Muscle Sliver with a QUEENIE to boot! A 15-damage turn was had once the Queen hit the board. 

NFLY. Again stifled by mana-shortage, No Landia is letting the dangerous Slivers back into this series. They simply cannot move fast enough to contend with this group, suddenly.

NLFY. The illustrious No Landia advances to their second consecutive finals berth!! The game was dominated by a Black Vise - without it, they would've struggled. There were 2x Vise by the end, but the first one that hit the battlefield on the initial turn was responsible for 17-damage. They destroyed two lands to suffocate the Slivers and, with a trusty Terror to kill a Winged Sliver, 2x Dark Ritual and a Drain Life, it propelled them to victory.

Slivers. What a disappointing finish. Seeing the Vise on the first turn was nearly doomsday - they aren't equipped to fend off the artifacts and were too slow out the gate to overcompensate. Instead, they were pummeled turn after turn for the lion's share of their damage. Really felt like their year!!!!

Dopes. Designed well for the Glop Artists, the Dopes were able to sit back and wait for the offense to come to them. They stole creature after creature and met very little resistance.

Artists. Their only damage outside of one attacking Serra were two Storm Seeker's -- not that great of a card. There's a LOT of waiting around by this team, still. Need to think thru their roster to see what other flexibility they might have.

Slivers. Three different dual lands gave way to 5 different Slivers. Once Crystalline hit the battlefield it was over for Darwin. The Hurricane they had in-hand became useless. Nice win by the Slivers to start the season.

Darwin. Start their 6th season in disjointed fashion against one of the original ten (O-10). Fallow Wurm showed a huge weakness - the high likelihood that you simply cannot summon the beast. What good is a 4/4 at 3MC if you can't put it in play? No good. Might need a Djinn substitute or two.

Stormy. This is just a very good team. They deployed 7 cards for 24-damage - two Ball Lightning's for half and the rest DD spells. They had the mana to sprint in this one. Killing off a Shocker early on helped too.

Gloves. A 1st-turn Underworld Dreams is a great way to start but they didn't generate much in the way of help afterward. Two-headed Giant sat in-hand -- not sure if he belongs in this squad.

Potatoes. This is a very good team. With one Land Tax, they pummeled NLFY with Armageddon after Armageddon until they had nothing left. The Potatoes win this game with 1 creature in play (Tundra Wolves) who was aided late by two Mishra's Factory. That's, still, insane. 

NLFY. Going second hurt, but they still put a Black Vise out on their first turn. That was about the last time things looked good for them. They had mana, but no creatures and the M-Kill being cast felt like a cool breeze by the time it hit the Potatoes, thanks to Land Tax. Going to be a challenging series!

Potatoes. Same recipe as the first game, if a bit delayed. They were in the driver seat by the 8th turn when a N-Disk wiped the board clean, but this was already after an Armageddon had decimated NLFY mana supply once. They withstood five of their own lands getting destroyed, and another early Black Vise. Most damage was done by Tundra Wolves again, then Kjeldoran foot soldiers. 

NLFY. Not looking good. They drew well again here and took an early lead behind a Vise, but couldn't withstand the Armageddon/Land Tax/Orb combo. They have no way to recover. They did a nice job destroyed opposing mana supply (5x) but had very little offense afterward.

Potatoes. Three mana is all it took. First turn Wolves, then two more on the second turn, then a Crusade on the third. A Cleansing helped ensure a faster victory but it was largely a no contest win.

NLFY. Drew 7-lands in first 12 cards with nothing but their heavy hitters - 3x Vampire and 2x Hordes. Went down quickly here and are now on the brink of elimination.

Potatoes. Champions!!!! And what a finale it was - 29-turns of punches thrown with over 30 cards deployed en route to victory. They destroyed double-digit lands in this game and lost 14 of their own, which is partly what fueled the victory (See: Zuran Orb). The Land Tax / Orb / Armageddon combo was fully dressed for this series, controlling a number of games en route to a sweep. The Potatoes managed to win with only 4 creatures played the entire game - highly unusual. They are champs for the 2nd time in 3 years, an unprecedented feat for the league. Congrats to Smashed Potatoes for completing their dominance as the #1 seed thru to the championship.

NLFY. They looked like a JV Team out there. No chemistry was had in this game, as their reliance on Black Vise to go on the offensive proved fruitless here, as they were no match for the superior Potatoes. After getting manhandled in the past game in five turns, they at least put up a serious fight here, going toe-to-toe with the champ for 29 turns (2nd longest ever). For the first time in a while it looks like No Landia will need to shake up its' roster.

Slivers. Ah-ha! They can beat this team after all! Not one, or two but THREE Sliver Queen's made it onto the battlefield in this one. They dealt themselves quite a bit of damage with City of Brass to cast them (6-damage), but it was the only way they were going to win this game. Nicely done.

Things. Had nothing in this one. Survived the first two Queen's with a Counterspell and Living Death but they had little else to compete. One Hidden Horror was left stranded in-hand with only one other creature around; Nether Shadow. They'll look forward to the next match-up.

NLFY. Win a close one here thanks to two Nevinyrral's Disk. The second cleared out a Serra and Mirri to save them from defeat. Two Sengir Vampires closed it out.

Artists. Ya know, this was a good one for them. A very tough opponent and they battled them well. Signing 4x Fog was huge, and so was the addition of a few Disenchant's. It helped them stave off the attack BUT a Meekstone would have done the same thing. Hard to tell if that switch was a good one :(

Stormy. Thanks to 8DD spells, the Stormy's push to 3-1. Key to the victory was dispatching 'Ol Yawggy mid-game. A Balduvian Horde helped too.

Team 11. So close. Don't know that I'm playing them correctly yet, but they had two Su-Chi's killed early, then recovered with a Yawgmoth Demon thanks to two Mana Vault's. One more turn would've helped a Titania's Song put Basalt Monolith and Puppet Strings into play. Not sure what the supplemental cards need to be but this was close.

Fault. Damn, this team is dirty. On turn-4, they were sitting pretty with a Hyppy and a Shocker but little else, and decided to deploy a Dark Ritual and a Wheel of Fortune. As expected, the WoF brought good fortune - a Megrim (played w/ earlier Dark Ritual), a Strip Mine (used to destroy a Maze of Ith) and a HTT. Factor in an attack, and the Fault ended up doing 23-damage in one turn (15-shocker, 4-hyppy, 4-HTT). Destruction.

Hot. Suffered from some early mana issues and never had a chance to claw back following the utter decimation on turn-5.

Things. First turn Dark Ritual into a Hidden Horror always helps. A few turns later saw a Unstable Mutation pump up the Horror, and a Nether Shadow to go with it. Yeesh.

Artists. Stuck on two lands and had no answer for the Horror. Bloated hand with 3 Serra Angel's, a Storm Seeker and a few unhelpfuls; Regrowth, Disenchant, Winter Orb.

Stormy. What a win!!!! Man oh man did the sky fall in this one. They poured on some serious heat with 9 direct damage spells, disposing of life and creatures (two Shocker's and a Hyppy) en route to a super-slim victory. Their last draw, a Shock, was the clincher. Good thing, they were toast next turn.

Fault. Destroy six lands in this one and were SO close after playing a Pox/Terror to flip two Rukh's on the board and knock the Stormy's down to four life. This game was as good as done until Stormy drew the game MVP.

Team 11. Solid win and the first of the season!!! They drew very nicely, starting with ample mana, an Assassin, a Mana Vault, Black Vise, Winter Orb, Icy, two Su-chi's and a Titania's Song. It's precisely the arrangement of cards needed to put some pressure on the opponent in a funky way. The Tranquility saved the day, erasing a soon-to-be-deadly combo of Manabarbs and Power Surge, along with a Propoganda. Team 11 was wasting away until they drew the Tranquility. May be hope for this team yet! 

Icy Hot. Looked devoid of offense here. They didn't have the counters to keep Team 11 contained, and almost subsided on the aforementioned combo, but couldn't get there. Two Wall's were killed off, but they were otherwise creatureless.

NLFY. Classic play by NLFY. They looked like they were heading for the exits midway thru the game, then turned it on late. They end up hanging on thanks to game-MVP Icy M to stall their mana. A hefty frontcourt of Sengir Vampire and Demonic Hordes took care of the rest. A Hellfire was critical in dispatching two Crystalline Slivers.

Slivers. Lacked offensive punch in this game. All four of their creatures, save a late S'Queen, was dispatched during the game. Even if they had access to their own mana, it may not have mattered. Tough loss to divisional O-10 foe in NLFY.

Potatoes. Disenchant saves their asses in this one. Can't wait for Round 2. Subsided on two Crusade's who pumped up four creatures + 2 Mishra's Factory Workers. 

Gloves. Sat on three Black Vise's, two Howling Mines and an Underworld Dreams when they went down. Would have won this thing had they not lost a Dreams earlier. Gonna be a fun match-up :)

Doom. HELL yeah! A first-turn Fastbond enables four lands, a Sol Ring and a Ernham. Next turn, Ernham. This thing was over in 4-turns. 

Dopes. Looked like Dopes in this one - summoned nothing on two lands. Don't know what their destiny is yet but this isn't looking good.

Stormy. Win their first match-up with the Artists easily thanks to multiple DD spells and two Ball Lightning's. Detonate takes MVP as it dispatched a pesky Winter Orb early and changed the dynamic of the game.

Artists. Had some mana issues for most of the game, but just didn't put much pressure on the Stormy's. Next few cards in-hand were two mana, a Mask and an Angel. If only they could've delayed a bit more.

Fault. Real gutsy win here. They fall down early, partly due to mana issues, as the Things jump out to a 20-7 lead. Then, with three straight M-Kill cards, they unleash two Megrim's and two Mind Twist's in a row for 28 total damage and the win. Really nice comeback here.

Things. Saw some early synergy between STDevils and two Unstable Mutation's to knock the Fault back into a big hole. But mana issues plagued their ability to do much else. Good to see the new additions give a different angle on the Things, though.

11. Incredible win!!! They take down the current #1 see in Sliver Lining in a gritty gritty win. Ample mana (four lands, two Birds and a Mana Vault) pushed Su-Chi and Ol Yawggy into the battlefield. A Winter Orb helped freeze the board for a turn, and an Icy sealed the deal. Impressive win as the fought a swarm of 5 Slivers. Impressive win against a great team, really showed some signs here.

Slivers. Just dumbfounded. Lose to a bottom dweller despite a nice show of force on the battlefield. Some C-Kill would've changed their fate. They'll look forward to Round 2 in Week 16.

NLFY. Demonstrative victory, totally great down the Hot thanks to a deadly combo Black Vise, Cyclopean Tomb and Icy Manipulator. The Hot were on ice fast without a move to make thanks to a mana pool full of Swamps.

Icy Hot. Looked dreadful. Summoned one non-Artifact all game. Had they even two untouched Islands, this game may have gone differently given their hand full of bounce.

Potatoes. Ugly game featuring next to no offense for either side. Battle of attrition that Potatoes won. Glad this one's over as the clock strikes 10PM :) 

Dopes. Really have nothing going. They're not a good team, or even really a team. It's an assembly of cards that aim to thwart the opponent by prolonging the game and forcing a mirrored attack. Need an injection of new life here.

Doom. No contest in this one. Crushed the Gloves with a Yavimaya Ants (13pts), two Archers and a Hurricane (4pts).

Gloves. No mana doomed them from the start.

Things. Great victory against their division rival. They stormed out with a STDevil and three Unstable Mutations to pump him to a 8/8 at one point. Knocking out the meddlesome creature took three DD spells from DnS thanks to Counterspell. A few turns later, though, and the Things had reloaded with another STDevil, a Hidden Horror and a a resurrected Nether Shadow. Nice win with their pesky creatures - a rarity!

DnS. If not for the Counterspell, the Storm take this one home. Close, but hella fun contest.

Artists. Win a make-up game in this one, and much in part to new addition Swords to Plowshares who killed off a Hyppy which wouldn't have made it impossible for the disguised Dreadnougth to attack. The Argivian Archaeologist saw some action and saved the day after the Nought was killed. Just love the Mask-Nought combo. So great.

Fault. Drew 3 Swamps all game despite a hand full of red M-Kill. They are a weaker club now, and it's uncertain where their offense will come from when the M-Kill isn't there.

Slivers. Total beat down thanks to 5 slivers and a Coat of Arms. The final play saw 18-damage done thanks to three 6/6s. Yeesh. Nothing prettier than a bunch of gold cards summoned by a trio of dual lands. Pritteeee goood.

Hot. Looked terrible. Crystalline Slivers nullified any bounce, and they certainly didn't have the creatures to contend. Looked bad enough to dismantle in this one.

NLFY. No contest, but a funny win. A first turn Dark Ritual throws two Vise's on the table for immediate punishment. A few turns later gave way to another Vise via Demonic Tutor for 18-damage to cap the game in 5-turns. Nice.

Team 11. Couldn't draw a goddamn thing. Mulligan 3x to start, then have their lone Swamp destroyed en route to certain defeat. Maybe next time!

Potatoes. Great showing of offense and defense in this game, though they were down early. A HUGE move to play Balance at around turn-8 completely shifted the momentum. They were in control from that point, with a Cleansing and a Wrath of God still to come. Powerhouse team.

Darwin. Were on track to win this one until the Balance. Had a Force and a Stream of Life in hand with ample mana to win. Post-Balance, they drew nothing but lands, and were toast.

Gloves. Fun to see Terferi's Puzzle take over the game. Hands were turned over over 5x, at least in the win, granting 3x Vise's, two Howling Mine's and a few other fun one's en route to an eventual U-Dreams. Creatureless for the win. Love it.

Dopes. Looked the part. This team needs to be altered mightily, just not sure wha tit is yet.

Things. Nice win against the newly revamped Extinction. Two big players here were an early STDevils + Unstable Mutation which dealt 10 damage, then the Spirit of the Night that came out via "Certain Death" combo. 

Extinction. Not bad!! Early Kird Ape, some BoPs and they had the makings of something! Not a ton of firepower in this game but that's OK.

Fault. Great comeback. After starting out down 9pts, they stormed back on the heels of three Rukh Egg's - two of which were turned into Rukh's thanks to two Terror's. An early Mind Twist helped remove two Mountains and an Incinerate, too. Rare that they get a win entirely from their creature base!

Stormy. Ran out of firepower. They couldn't deal with the flying Rukh's in creature defense or more DD. They played with concern about discarding cards, though maybe that backfired here.

Team 11. OH YEAH! Take down the #1 seed and divisional foe with only two lands to their name! Awesome win. They put a Llanowar Elf in play, then a Mana Vault, then a Su-Chi, then two Basalt Monolith's. A turn later they turned all of them into creatures with MVP Titania's Song. It was a beatdown after that. SO fun to see the team come together. Check.

NLFY. Were outgunned in this one. They destroyed two lands and didn't draw any Swamps afterward. Thought hard about the first Strip Mine used on turn-1, but who's to say they wouldn't have drawn more lands at that point. Next time!

Sliver. Slide by in a no-contest. Ratchet up to 4 Slivers to dole out a whopping 47 total damage.

Hot. Drew terribly in this game - no defense to speak of against a creature heavy outfit. Next time!

Monsters. The roster revamp provides INSTANT IMPACT against the #2 Team in the league. With only TWO lands for most of the game, the Monsters withstand some defense, throw out three Dreadnought's and two Mask's en route to a demonstrative 26pt victory. We can't pencil their names into any playoff race yet, but a nice sign from Game 1.

Potatoes. Whoa, didn't see that coming. A six game win streak is put to an end thanks to some strong draw-power of the Monsters. Let's just say they've circled their revenge game in a few weeks (W13).

Doom. Good win with only 3 lands. Their first play of the game was their biggest - dispatching a BVise with recent signing; Crumble. They were able to put some meat on the table (Elvish Archers, Djinn) and go to work. A late Winter Blast, also a recent signing, pushed a brutish Giant out of the way for the victory. Good win.

Gloves. Not quite there. They were lagging in this one despite putting a Howling Mine and U-Dreams into play. They definitely need more defense while they build up their attack so will have to think about some changes.

Stormy. Call it, 1-2-3 Ball Lightning dole out 18-damage in a thunderstorm of a win.A Fork'd Incinerate took the finishing move for the Stormy to bounce back after two straight losses.

X. A difficult opponent - three creatures disposed (Kird Ape & Lions). Not sure Lightning Bolt fits into this deck. Need something more formidable - on O or D.

Fault. A very nice win. They benefited from the Things mana surplus - didn't have to deal with much of an attack outside of one Necrosavant, and a Pox took care of him while also handing out double-digit damage to set up the win. I still love the Megrim-Wheel of Fortune-Hypnotic Specter-Pox synergy in this deck. It's almost rhythmic.

Things. Unusual game for them - drew two creatures and no G-Pump all game. Were sitting ducks until the end.

Sliver. Total blowout thanks largely to an early Disenchant. It destroyed a Basalt Monolith that was primed and ready to put a Yawggy on the battlefield in the next turn. Four slivers (Muscle, Winged, Heart and Crystalline) were buffed by a Coat of Arms and laid havoc to T-11.

Team 11. Had their stars aligned early until Disenchant. They were stuck in the mud afterward.

NLFY. No contest. Tho they did put out their best turn-1 move yet - a Swamp into Sol Ring into two Black Vise. OoOoOoO da lolly!

Hot. No mana spells death against his team.They drew there, and made poor choices after that summoning creature defense instead of relying on counters. This team is brutal.

Potatoes. Fought off 3 Black VIse, two Howling Mine and a soon-to-be-massive Entropic Specter for the win. Timely Swords to Plowshares on the Specter was huge, as was an early Disenchant of one of the Vise's, and the weenie's did the rest. 

Gloves. I still love playing with this team but they really don't have any defense outside of some creatures (that they can't always put out in play) and a board-clearing Jokulhaups. Need to noodle on this to make them more competitive.

Monsters. Well well! Proving last week's win over the Potatoes was no aberration! Most damage was done by a pumped up Shyft, courtesy of new FA; Phantasmal Mount. A turn-10 Counterspell saved them tho, keeping a game-ending Yavimaya Ants from hitting the battlefield. Great win.

Doom. Win without the Counterspell, but were hurt by the casting speed of their defensive cards like Hurricane and Winter Blast (both sorceries). An early Crumble kept a Mask from staying on the field tho - huge pick up.

Extinction. Big win for the the up-and-coming X. They relied on Savannah Lions to dole out most of their damage, and had some help from a late Fireball as well. 

Fault. Strange game, the discard players came first, then the Megrim. They were already taking heat from the Lions and reset with a WoF, but it didn't bring the help needed.

DnS. Bringing the rain! A statement game against the Things to further thrust them into the League's elite. They had some FIREPOWER in this one, tossing out two Bolts, an Incinerate, a Chain Lightning, a Ball Lightning and a Fireball for the win. Smokey, smokey, smokey.

Things. Tough draw, landing no creature cards. Were able to muster a Spirit of the Night into play but it was way too late.

Team 11. Grueling victory. Many Winter Orb's meet a Manabarbs and the lassitude settled in. They win this game with only 4 drawn lands in 17 turns - absurd, but speaks to how low-cost they are. Priest of Yawgmoth was critical in this game, turning a Winter Orb into the Icy Manipulator that ended up winning the game thanks to Titania's Song.

Hot. Terrible team, felt like they sat around all game waiting to be extinguished. Needs to be salvaged for parts..

Sliver. Hang on to win a long against divisional foe; NLFY. An early Disenchant smoked out a N'Disk, clearing the way for muchos Slivers to hit he field and wreak havoc. 

NLFY. Just couldn't get there. Saw the tide turning a little over halfway thru the game, in their favor, too, but then had two Vampire's tossed out via StP, and just couldn't recover.

Potatoes. Faster, stronger and captured the win easily against a rallying Doom. Two Crusade helped pump a Wolves and a Archer.

Doom. Couldn't respond fast enough to Potatoes offensive. They had mana, creatures but just couldn't get there.

Gloves. A nice, nice win by the Gloves!! Two Howling Mines, a Vise, a Winds of Change to pull a U-Dreams out to compliment an Entropic Specter. Monsters couldn't get much out in this game.

Monsters. Slow game, they had no offense or defense. Still, the anticipation with each draw is as high as any other team in history. So fun.

Extinction. Win a game they should have won. A Erhnam Djinn does most of the work helping to put 5 creatures in the graveyard. Two Lhurgoyf were pumped up as a result.

Things. Two mana had them stranded.

Stormy. Smackdown! Three Ball Lightning and a few DD spells did the trick. A well-timed Earthquake disposed of two Shocker also.

Fault. Two mana stifled them.

Slivers. Know what they have to do to put pressure on NLFY and executed wonderfully. Four Slivers in play dealt twenty damage in 5 turns. Too fast for No Landia.

NLFY. Pace was too fast for them. Had a N-Disk in play but needed one more turn to eradicate the Sliver slew.

Doom. A game that really showcased the value of Elvish Ranger. Unencumbered, the Ranger decimated the Monsters in a few short turns. The game changer though, was Crumble. It was drawn the turn after a Dreadnought was played (turn-3) and completely swung the game.

Monsters. Just unlucky. A Brainstorm into the Mask into a Nought-Relic Barrier combo. This game was close to over on their third turn until Crumble saved the day. Not a bad team though.

Gloves. Unconventional but great win for the Gloves. An early Entropic Spector did most of the dirty work. No help from the hand shuffling scheme but benefited from a glut of mana from the Potatoes.

Potatoes. Poor showing. Drew no creature defense in this game and were at the mercy of the Spector as a result.

Fault. Put the pressure on early with a Shocker dealing 13-damage in one turn thanks to a Dark Ritual into Megrim. Were kept at bay despite doubling Megrim and Shocker, but won it with a Hyppy who couldn't be blocked.

Things. Didn't have much of any offense this game. Deserved to lose.

Stormy. Perfect antidote to Extinction, destroying three of four creatures with DD. Two Ball Lightning, an Earthquake, an Incinerate and a Fireball did the rest. I like Earthquake on this team.

Extinction. Still missing something on this team and I'm not sure what it is - maybe even more creatures (Mtenda Lions). Lotta lands with nothing to summon against a creatureless team.

Slivers. No contest after killing Su-Chi.

11. One swamp killed them.

NLFY. No contest.

Hot. Stink.

Potatoes. Lose this game, fast, without two Plowshares who took care of back-to-back Noughts. A trio of Tundra Wolves took care of the rest - in part due to a Crusade. Armageddon always kept Monsters from countering anything.

Monsters. Tough loss. Had all the makings of a beat down but the Potatoes had the only two cards that could have saved them.

Gloves. A WILD finish! The game was over, the final count we reverting taken until an error had been detected. Teferi's Puzzle Box occurs 2x when there are two in play - a big learning. As a result, a desperation move by the Gloves to use a Wheel of Fortune ends up winning the game by drawing a Dark Ritual and a Puzzle Box. They go into play to join another Box, a Howling Mine and a U-Dreams. The Wheel caused 7-damage, then the two Box and Mine caused another 13-damage. That's 20-damage in one turn!!!! This is precisely why this deck was built 👍

Darwin. Have the victory ripped out from under them in devastating fashion. What a finish.

Things. Nothing like grabbing a W by casting only two cards. Living Death is just deadly.

Stormy. Needed a bit more offense to win this one.

Slivers. No contest. A Queen sighting though!!!

Hot. Stink.

NLFY. Redemption can be a sweet song. They destroy four mana producers to totally disable Team 11 and ride two Vise to victory. This is a very good, simple team.

Team 11. Couldn't get off the ground after losing three lands and one Elves. Tough loss.

Potatoes. Hung around, knocked out two creatures with Balance, then used an Outpost and duo of Factory to win the game.

Doom. Their game to lose. Sat on two Library, giving them 5 cards to reveal per turn and were stuck with five lands at the top of their library.

Gloves. Train is rolling - four in a row now! Serious manipulation of the Monsters here as they flipped the hands more than a few times with Winds of Change. One of them was used after the Monsters pulled a Mask into their hand with a Nought in the way. A U-Dreams ground them away down to rubble.

Monsters. The Field of Dreams-Millstone combo is nice and helps protect them as intended. Still taking a while to find the deadly combo but it still looks okay.

Fault. An incredible comeback. They were down 20 to 2 and crawled all the way back to victory. They destroyed 6 total lands, used a Tutor to pull a game saving Megrim, and put a Pox in play that shifted the balance. Great win.

Stormy. Threw 7DD spells at them and came up short. Losing lands was critical as two Ball Lightning we're stranded time and time again. Came down to the wire.

Extinction. Great win! A timely Giant Growth helps a Lhurgoyf extinguish a massively pumped Nether Shadow to keep their hopes alive and eventually close out. Another Goyf came, as did another Ape. Great win.

Things. Ridiculous string of Unstable Mutation to pump up the Shadow 3x. Otherwise, though, they didn't have much with three Buried Alive in their hand which would have pumped the opposing Goyf.

NLFY. Cruise to ready victory after destroying two of 11s three lands.

11. Had nothing in this one.

Hot. Wow, just when they had been promised dead! The Hot pull a fast one on the league's top team by sitting on a Power Surge all the way to victory! Wall of Great was critical in stopping there Sliver attack, and they just sat there.

Slivers. Pull three creatures and half their mana against the one team that encourages you to use mana. What a snafu!

Doom. Wow, crawl out with a victory thanks to two Instant spells on their final turn; Crumble and Giant Growth. The later helped pump a Elves earlier in the game as well, as Darwin ends a 4-game winning streak for the Gloves.

Gloves. Dang, their early Winds of Change was huge, giving them two more U-Dreams, a Vise and two Shocker. Ran out of time thanks to the Y-Ants. Their own Howling Mine enabling there Giant Growth that won Darwin the game. Might need some C-Kill in this deck.

Things. Great revenge game for the underdog Things. They put 20-points of damage on in two turns thanks to a Horror and Kraken.

Fault. Once the big guy hit the graveyard on turn-3, it wasn't looking good. They had no C-Kill, and could only hope to discard key cards.

Extinction. Nice win, use two Lion, an Ape and a Djinn to take it home. Big Worldly Tutor to bring out the Djinn.

Stormy. Tough game, couldn't do much but wait for not mountains.

Sliver. Acidic Sliver wins the game for the Slivers who remove a Metallic Sliver to kill off a Assassin.

11. Didn't have much here, may not be built for the fast opponent.

Hot. Second win in a row!! Two Control Magic win them two Vampire, and the dual Tower pumped then to record high in life. Can't believe they beat two top teams in a row.

NLFY. Shocked! Had an early Black Knight killed, then both Vampire stolen. No other offense despite killing off most of opposing mana supply.

Potatoes. No contest. Two White Knight completely dismantle the Gloves.

Gloves. No defense against COP Black creatures. Hmmmm.

Monsters. Three Maze of Ith save the day! Deadly Nought makes it it on 7th turn, aided by a Phantasmal Mount, and wins the game on the 8th turn.

Doom. Locked and loaded in this one but came up short. Eight creatures hit the battlefield but we're stymied by the Maze

Extinction. Might have been the best game of the season. Their game plan really came into effect here, outlasting there land destruction from BAF with three Birds, then subsiding on Chaosphere and two Angelic Renewal to take down a Rukh, then power Mirri to do here thing. A late Lhurgoyf joined the fray as well at 5/6. A first turn Worldly Tutor was big to bring in a Birds. They lost six lands and still came from behind to win.

Fault. A little more offense and they have this wrapped up. Summoned only three creatures, and all there were killed off. The Megrim in play did nothing which was odd given how much mana was destroyed - X was just that fast.

Stormy. Win a big one thanks to a board clearing Earthquake, Mana struggles for the Things and a Horde. Balance shifted a few times.

Things. Needed this one to gain some ground in the middle of the pack.

Sliver. Boom baby! Dispatch NLFY as fast as they arrived, using a City of Brass to power five slivers en route to s shellacking. The Heart Sliver and Muscle Sliver played big parts.

Landia. Drew a Mulligan to start and never recovered. Couldn't kill the City of Brass and were too far behind to defend the Sliver attack.

Hot. Incredible!! A third win in a row!!!! Stealthy play using bounce cards and counters to keep Team 11 from completing an attack were huge. Propaganda was key paired with a Manabarbs, and that was that.

11. Tough loss. Too a lot of damage to get attackers in play and couldn't keep them there. Frustrating loss.

Darwin. Big win! Showed a nice blend of offense and defense in this game, dispatching two opposing creatures while attacking with a Djinn and a Force.

Potatoes. Imbalance of mana and three Land Tax left then weak. Tough loss.

Gloves. Outrageously fun game. The Gloves put the heat on with two Vise, two Mine,a Puzzle Box and a Underworld Dreams. Dealt seven critical damage in the last turn to win.

Monsters. I love this team. They're so close to being a tough opponent. Still have to work hard for their Ws but that's alright. Had a hidden Nought on the board but didn't have time to get him out.

Team 11. Drew well with two Vise in hand to immediately put the Things into single-digits. One more Vise and two Su-Chi later and this one was over. Came down to a critical last draw for the Things.

Things. If they had drawn a mana on the last play, they win. They didn't, so down they went! Two Buried Alive put a bevy of stoutly blokes in the graveyard ready for resuscitation.

Potatoes. Win a big game to stave off the encroaching 3 seed. Land destruction was key - Armageddon killed off five mana to turn the game. Zuran Orb wins MVP for granting an additional 14 life to keep them alive.

Stormy. Use all four Ball Lightning and several DD spells but just couldn't rebound after the Potatoes drew a second Orb.

NLFY. Wins easily thanks to an Icy. It disabled Fault and paved a path to victory behind 2 Royal Assassin, a Sengir and a Demonic Hordes. Easy win once the locks were in place.

Fault. Stumbled out the gate. Didn't have enough offense, defense or anything to keep them in this.

Monsters. Drew very well - Mask, Nought, Barrier, Meekstone. They were out in front almost without laying down a card. The mix is working, I think. I like the synergies of throwing Ornithopter in there as well. Not sure about Phantasmal Mount but it's ok for now.

Hot. Had nothing going, though they did steal the Nought!

Extinction. BEAUTIFUL win. They drew two lands all game - TWO - and carefully, meticulously pulled Doom apart. Outstanding win. A key difference maker was new FA Kudzu! Destroying two lands! Two BoPs and a Sol Ring kept X afloat with a Lhurgoyf that pushed them to victory. Great win. Dispatched 4 opposing creatures, too. Had two WoGs in hand also. Awesome win.

Doom. Lost the upper hand once Kudzu entered the field. They sat on a Ants, a Ranger and some more defense.

Gloves. Keep this train rolling!!! The Gloves take out the #1-seed in style with a Vise, two U-Dreams, a Howling Mine with an Entropic Specter and a Wil-o on the frontline. Great win.

Slivers. Tough loss, had no E-Kill and it cost them. Did put a Sliver Queenie in play which, of course, is always fun.

Doom. Drew exceptionally well to thwart 10s impending attack. Two defensive cards - Tranquility & Verdigris - we're critical in paving the way for a Force of Nature.

10. Had 11 mana on their second turn thanks to three Mana Vault's. Crazy. Had Planar Gate destroyed early which really hurt them. A late Yawggy was there but no match for the big boy in green. Royal Assassin was left stranded in hand too. Juuuuust about there!

Potatoes. Win a battle of attrition against their rival. Used a Balance to knock out opposing lands and a Sengir Vampire. FAs like Land Tax and Cleansing really hamper NLFY. They could rely on cheaper creatures much more so than NLFY could, and win as a result.

NLFY. Amazing they were even able to dole out 11-damage in this one. They have real problems with the Potatoes new emphasis on land destruction, life pump and mana pump. Balance may have shifted toward the Potatoes in this match-up of Original 10 teams.

Monsters. Nice win. They drew well with a Mask-Nought in the initial hand, then had protection with multiple counters as they played chicken with 3 disguised Ornithopter. Two Grave-Res were key to retrieve the fallen Nought mid-game.

Things. Were quick and nimble with two Shadow and two Devils but didn't have any defense for the Nought.

Hot. Sat back and waited - just like at the end of S5. Propaganda and Mana Leak helped hugely, and Maze of Ith was valuable too. Rathi came in late to clean up the rest. 

Glop. Lotta wheel spinning for them. Ton of mana, but no offense. They were sitting and waiting too but outside of a Storm Seeker they had no way to deal damage. We'll see if that continues to be a problem.

Stormy. Cards like Earthquake and Pyroclasm are total game-changers against the Slivers. They used both in this game and extinguished three opponents. Slivers put out a Furnace of Rath that would have ended the game a turn later. Stormy had two draws left before going down to a Muscle Sliver - the third of the game - and pulled a Ball Lightning. Nice.

Slivers. Lost by the skin of their teeth. Lost 6 of 7 creatures in this game in a total card draw-down. One of the best games of the year.

Gloves. WHOA NELLY! Great win by the Gloves! Their synergy was off the charts, playing defense with two pesky Wisp to keep two Shocker at bay while two Howling Mine, two U-World Dreams and a Black Vise closed things out. They won this game on the last draw of the game, putting a second Mine, Dreams and the Vise into play which dealt 7 damage to put the Fault away. Great game.

Fault. So close, but what a match-up. These two teams are exact opposites and it's OUTSTANDING to pit them against one another. Had this wrapped up with one turn remaining. Lost a key Mind Twist earlier in the game, too.

Fault. Nice win, just eroding the Potatoes hand with the Hyppy. They didn't have much else but were able to destroy a few lands that happy them in the clear.

Potatoes. Rough game without mana. Puts them into a battle for the #1 seed with the Slivers in the final week of the season.

Team 11. Incredible win - a Su-Chi flanked by two Elves and a Yawgmoth Priest took care of the near defenseless Gloves. A Winter Orb down the stretch proved pivotal, as it froze out the opponent from a playing a game-ending Sudden Impact.

Gloves. SO CLOSE! The ridiculous M-Pump of Team 11 (3x Mana Matrix, two Elves, Priest) disabled three Black Vise despite sitting on a Howling Mine - really incredible loss vs. it being an incredible win for the 11.

NLFY. Demonic Hordes were critical in disabling the Monsters. The game also heavily changed when Hellfire scratched two Noughts from the field.

Monsters. Roster is solid but it's missing something. Not much creature defense available.

Doom. Nonchalant win facing very little pressure outside of initial STDevils. 

Things. Drew more lands than spells cast.

What's At Stake -- If the Slivers win, they'll have pulled 0.5 games behind the Potatoes with one game left to go. If they beat BAF in the final game, they have a shot at the regular season title. 

If the Potatoes win, the #1-seed is theirs!

Gloves. So fun!! The synergy on this team was awesome. They played one creature all game - Shocker - and he recorded not one point of damage. Black Vise took the first load, then a second Underworld Dreams paired exceptionally well with a Winds of Change and Teferi's Puzzle Box to knock out the Artists. What a fun team.

Artists. Had a tough time getting going with all the hand shuffling. Tough opponent for them because their mana manipulation didn't help much.

What's at Stake:

Doom. If they can win here, they increase their chances of bypassing the first round of the Qualifier Playoff AND to possibly grab the #7-seed vs. #8.

Hot. Nothing, they're terrible.

Stormy. Patience paid off here. They held as many DD spells as possible for the Mask'ed Noughts and when they came, they were gone just as fast. A Meekstone slowed down their creature-led assault a bit, but it was only a matter of time.

Monsters. Looked VERY one dimensional in this game. May need to drop the updated rules for Mask-Nought to give them more heft. It's proving difficult a combo to build around as we near another down season with these two at the core.

Things. Turn 1 Buried Alive. Turn 3 Living Death. Turn 4 twenty-eight damage. Turn 5 eighteen damage. SEEEEEE YA Hot.

Hot. Welp, that was brutal.

Sliver Lining. BOOOOOOM BABY! That's a smackdown right there. The Fault draw nothing of interest and the Slivers slap them back into concrete.

Fault. LIMPING into the playoffs after this dismantling. Almost need the Qualifier to find some momentum, but they don't have the luxury...

NLFY. Ugly game won entirely by a Black Vise. Literally nothing else happened. These guys are cruising for the playoffs.

Stormy. Mana shortage hurt them for the L.

X. Sat tight on a Savannah Lion and THREE Giant Growth for the win. Pushes their win streak to 7 in a row to head into the playoffs. Awesome turnaround.

Team 11. Needed a bit more mana to put some ammo out there.

narrative.preview: We're back!! That's right! We're back baby!! Season 7 is upon us at last! Once again, The League is expanding - this time to 16 teams. Armed to the teeth with battlefield accoutrements, we see the much heralded return of several Original 10 Teams -- Thrull Frottle, Fire Drilled, Team 10 -- who have been revamped for combat. The Drillers play off the former mana surplus strategy while Team 10 puts 'Ol Yawggy and Winter Orb back together and the Thrulls, well, they remain unchanged..? Then we see a new entrant duo -- Clockwork & YCBI. Machine is the offspring of Team 11, building entirely around Titania's Song, defense and some absolute bruisers. YCBI is an honorary nod to Dad's original Zephyr Falcon Team with a few strategic pillars thrown in, like Kismet and Meekstone. The Mist Monsters shift again, pairing the Mask-Nought combo with Field of Dreams and a host of millers to play D. This season features four divisions of four teams each. It's gonna be bananas. Also, the crowned champion of Season 7 will be thrust into cardboard battle with Boss Fight in a best of 5 series with initial life totals increased to 30 each.

teams.writeup: Building a roster around Titania's Song has proven difficult, but I think I learned another key reason why after playing with them in the playoffs; a 4CMC centerpiece card is hard to summon. With so many teams building mana-defense strategies, it becomes increasingly important for any team to keep a low-CMC profile. Portcullis falls into the same trap. So, Peek-a-Boom ends up waiting around strategy-less. It's actually sort of maddening! They also don't pass the 'fun to play with' test, which is kind of important. It's no wonder Peak-a-Boom is destined for the recycling bin as we close the books on Season 7.

This is perhaps the most confounding team in the history of the League. They put up a paltry 5-15 regular season record then power right thru the Qualifier Bracket with wins over Peek-a-Boom (2-1) and Bad Ass Fault (3-0) before falling to one of the only teams that actually wants to constantly shuffle their library; Mist Monsters (4-1). Their 'boom or bust' style actually reminds me of Murphy's Law. When they draw well, they're nearly unstoppable. When they don't, they're a swift execution. The fun part, though, is how to maximize the value of their MVP; Underworld Dreams.

This one's easy; the Thrulls are 28-48 all-time after taking a 5-15 nosedive in Season 7. Putting them in 'The Crossroads' is borderline outrageous. Everything about this team suggests a teardown is imminent. Even the focus on Hecatomb is risky, frankly, and leaves them without much of a second move if/when it falters. Maybe it's not a centerpiece card after all. Lord of the Pit, on the other hand, is intended to be more of a fun gimmick yet actually had some run this season. What about diversifying the creature-base to move away from Thrulls-only to inject a little more versatility? One thing is for damn sure, they're absolutely terrible.

Here we are again, Drillers. You gave us a spirited run winning four straight (2-0 vs Thrulls) before running into the YCBI buzzsaw in Round 2. Any strength here should be given an asterisk as they seemed to excel against two teams that didn't focus on land destruction. YCBI turned out to be a perfect foil for them. Their ability to defend the Driller creatures (Meekstone + Castle), slow down mana supply (Kismet) and attack unopposed with flying creatures shifted the balance entirely in favor of YCBI by Game 3. One thing is for sure, this version of Fire Drilled - where they're all in on Mana Flare - isn't coming back. It's just too slow to compete.

So the summary is...DnS win nearly 50% of their games (9-11) yet lose a tight series in the first round against the #7-seeded NLFY. They struggled to find an identity, shuffling 30 cards in/out throughout the season. The secondary strategy beyond low-CMC smoke makers is still a question mark. Per game metrics vs. last season were confounding - more turns with more mana to summon many more cards and yet their damage per turn or per game are down considerably AND they're taking more heat. It's tempting to want to run this back with draw engines on the team, like Howling Mine, to ensure that the well of low-CMC direct damage spells never runs dry.

What has happened to the once mighty yet now faulty Fault? They fell out of the top-6 by W8 and staggered the rest of the way. This is somehow a defense-first team but they couldn't protect the rim against anyone, giving up a staggering 1.96 Damage per Turn. They shuffled out 25 players this season, a pretty high mark. The twist this time was to lean more heavily on M-Kill as a strategy even if it meant losing their own acreage. It didn't go too well, going 2-6 after W13. Megrim is such a devastating card, and the M-Kill strategy still somehow seems complementary. There are two angles I can see with this roster: swap out mana-kill cards for more hand-kill cards, or add even more mana-kill cards to further suffocate opponents.

It feels like an eternity ago that they won the Season 5 championship in 7-games against Darwin. The league has changed a lot since then. For one, nearly half the teams from that season have been dismantled. No Landia has made a few adaptations here and there, but they've gotten a bit slower by adding high-CMC cards like Demonic Hordes, Hellfire, Sengir Vampire and Nevinyrral's Disk. In this playoffs, it was clear they were too slow to really compete. Ironically, they don't really have a secondary strategy to complement their focus on land destruction. How can they get leaner, faster and more complementary to their core strategy?

I love this outfit and so want them to be better. Their regular season record and key offensive statistics are a bit misleading. They played in the weakest division - their three divisional contemporaries were each under .500 posting a combined 19-41 record in the regular season and a 1-8 playoff record. They did prove competitive outside of the division with some solid wins. They were one victory away from making noise in the second round of the premier bracket. YCBI made things difficult with their C-Freeze strategy and drew kindly in the final two games with both Archangel and Wrath of God. But Darwin is tough and the additions of Living Lands + Overrun are fantastic.

What a run! I have to eat my words this time having stated 'I'm not long on this team' in the Regular Season Recap. Oh man, did they prove me wrong, winning their way to the second round of the Premier Bracket. They went the distance against every opponent, beating a surging FD (3-2), then a spunky Darwin team (4-3) before going down to the #1-seeded Extinction (4-3). Their strategy was really effective, locking down opposing creatures with ease and taking their time to build an attack. Archangel was a massive free agent signing before the playoffs, winning Game MVP in 6 of 10 wins. He was an absolute bruiser to defend and most teams just couldn't do it. All in all, a pretty great season to build on.

A tale of two things! A solid regular season team quickly morphed into a ferocious playoff squad. The Things were very active on the free agent market just before their Round 3 match-up against Team 10. The artist formerly known as 'Big Gloves' was put out to pasture after going down to the Mist Monsters and the Things capitalized by ransacking the remains for several draw-advantage cards and a couple of bruisers (Crimson Hellkite, Thundermare). In their Round 3 series, they straight up haunted Team 10, winning 4 times in six tries by an average 17.75 points on only 6.25 turns per contest. They then ran into an absolute buzzsaw in Sliver Lining who won every game in five turns. This has never happened in the history of The League.

'They seem well-positioned to beat almost any type of team, except those featuring heavy creature defense.' This was one of the closing comments made in the Regular Season Recap. Who'd they play in the first round? A Swamp Things unit that had as many massive mutants as the irrepressible Boss Fight team. It was clear from the get-go that Team 10 would need a strong showing from Winter Orb to have a chance. It surfaced in one game, Game 5, which they won, but that was it. Spirit of the Night and its Protection from Black was tough to defend and the Things were frankly too fast. It's possible they need a bit more offensive firepower beside their rather lean line of fighters, or maybe they just need another go.

There's nothing quite like getting a little zany with team construction and seeing it work. The Machine had a heck of a season, going 11-3 after going whole hog into the mono-artifact scheme. Their regular season turnaround led to an incredibly arduous matchup against the Mist Monsters. In historic fashion, the Monsters took Game 1 in only three turns. They have an identity, though, and something fun to build on entering their first full season as an artifact only squad. They're chock full of robots who love to poke and prod. The unique thing is their combination of speed + heft. There's no limitations to what this group might throw onto the battlefield because of how much mana they can generate.

What a phenomenal season. Their second one as a team, but really the first when you consider how their dynamic duo - Illusionary Mask & Phyrexian Dreadnought - was played. Their playoff run was strong, with victories over the winner of the Qualifier Bracket #15 Big Gloves (4-1), then handled the surging #4 Clockwork (4-1) in the Quarterfinals thanks to Maze of Ith and their bruiser combo. In the Semi-finals they took a commanding 3-0 series lead against #1 Extinction before closing it out three games later. The Finals were a struggle, as they lost games due to 'too much mana' and 'idle play'. They rely heavily on Maze of Ith to neutralize opposing creatures, but a card like Crystalline Sliver completely neutralized it in the Finals.

Earlier exit than anticipated for big X. They fell to the Monsters in six games in the semifinals after starting down 3-0. This is a gritty team, no doubt, but they may have played down to the competition in the playoffs. After all, they were taken to seven games by massive underdog YCBI in the quarterfinals. In fact, Extinction played the lowest seed in both rounds and was beaten 7-6. Nevertheless, I find they are here to stay. Their roster is hell bent on outright destruction, and they're good at bringing the fiery rain, but maybe they could use some more offense. Nothing in the big CMC department, but just more tenacity upfront.

I just didn't think we'd get here, writing about the Smashed playoff run before a champ was crowned. The Potatoes were just so good this season, namely on the defensive end, but it was their offensive shortcomings that ruined their chances at a repeat. They amassed only 28-damage in their four game sweep against the Slivers in the semi-finals - that's 7 damage per game on average. Pitiful. The commentary from their losses echoed one thought; creature defense. They just couldn't contend with the super-fast attack of the Slivers. The League looks to be heading this way too, with perhaps 1-3 more Sliver copycats on the way. The Potatoes owned a 1-6 record against the Slivers this season.

Hell yeah. They did it!! Seven full seasons and they finally did it. In Season 1 they finished 2-8, and then 4-8 in Season 2. It's been a long, iterative road to win the crown. This team was real good in the playoffs, losing only 3x in wins against the revamped #8 Swamp Things (4-1) in the Quarterfinals, the defending champion Smashed Potatoes (4-0) in the Semifinals and then, of course, the bruising #5 Mist Monsters in the Finals. If you remove two outlier victories against the Potatoes, the Slivers averaged less than 6-turns per game (5.7) in playoff wins, including 6 wins in either 4 or 5 turns. That's crazy fast. In fact, in their series win against #8 Swamp Things, they averaged 5-turns per game. Really incredible. Another interesting playoff datapoint is the fact that this was yet another champion who finished under the increasingly relevant DTT champ line at a scant 1.19.

report_cards.mvp: Hecatomb OR Lord of the Pit
report_cards.writeup: This is an odd group, and I'm not quite sure what to make of them. On one hand, they started the season 1-11, ended up posting the 11th worst U-DPI in history and felt like the league's punching bag. On the other hand, they led the league, by far, in Deployed-W at only 4.80 meaning they worked less to get their Ws. Winning came one of two ways for this ugly bunch; weathering foes down with Hecatomb or a super-fast deployment of Lord of the Pit.

Have to say, I'm shocked about this one. Here I thought the Gloves could rebound, play some D and befuddle even the best teams with their constant hand shuffling. But no! Instead, the Big Gloves were historically bad. It didn't occur to me until a loss to the Slivers in W17 that the U-Dreams strategy can't drive but, rather, needs to ride shotgun.

report_cards.statmuse: 3-8 post-rebrand not counting Ws v #16 Thrull Frottle
report_cards.writeup: Icy Hot starts the season 0-6, pivots into Peak-a-Boom and finishes 6-8. Big success story until you look into those wins with half of them coming against the lowly Thrulls. Titania's Song feels like a tantalizing card with 'build around me' potential. But several teams have now built around it with little success.

The return of the Drillers was exciting yet met with an admission of guilt. The league has gotten faster every year and more teams are putting an emphasis on mana-defense specifically.
