They sidestepped the Land Tax write deftly, opting to play a much slower pace than usual to avoid enabling the Potatoes, and it worked! They survived 2x Armageddon and a Zuran Orb with six of their own, namely the Winged and Muscle to win by just 7pts.

Potatoes. Lose only their third game of the season. They got beat here, no question about it. They had very little defense for flying creatures, especially once their Wall of Swords was dispatched by ba StP. Great game.

Things. Fantastic game and great win. Things start with one island and a predominantly black hand. A Mystic Tutor fetches the Underground Sea, and a turn later a SotNight was in play wreaking havoc. Protection against black was pretty big in this game. Several turns later, a Polar Kraken made it out and that was it.

Gloves. Ya know, in one game the Gloves showed why they're so frail and yet so potent at the same time. They sat idly most of the game collecting life on two Ivory Tower. A T- Puzzle Box opened the doors to Underworld Dreams and 16-damage in an instant. A single turn can generate multiple hand shuffles and an onslaught of damage. This was the best I've played this team and it made me think it can still be really really good.

X. Another big win against a top dog making it 8 of their last 9. Really impressive stretch. This win was a combo of "good win" and "loss" by T-10. An early E-Djinn put pressure on Team 10 who had no defense outside of Elvish Archers and STDevils. A late-Lhurgoyf wasn't all that impressive until a Cataclysm showed up, cleaning out the battlefield to make the Big L a 5/6 with his Djinn buddy beside him (thanks, Angelic Renewal). Was over that turn.

Team 10. Stumble here, just outgunned early and they had no BigCre defense. X was able to put two Mana Birds out there so I'm not sure Winter Orb would've done much. They aren't really built to beat out other mighty mite teams...

Slivers. Demonstrative win to make it 3 in a row! They had enough mana to put a Heart, Muscle, Crystalline and 2x Queenies on the board for the win.

Gloves. The U-Dreams play has to be the secondary strategy, it's clear to me now. This team just waits around for the right cards to wreak havoc but they're too vulnerable in that time.

10. What a complete victory this was. They had to use a lot of their war chest to win this game; Black Vise for a few turns, Mishra's Factory for a few more, a Winter Orb and 3x Felwar Stone, BoP with a Unholy Strength, the Treefolk, a Elvish Archers and 'Ol Yawggy too. They summoned 10 cards in 9 turns and ON ONLY 3 LANDS. That's pretty unusual and impressive. Fun win!

YCBI. Weren't as fast but had some defense in hand until the Orb came out. They were stifled from that point on.

X. Had nearly their entire creature base wiped out by a double-Pyroclasm, but took advantage by lobbing a Lightning Bolt at the opposing Wall of Earth to give an Erhnam Djinn free reign to bring home the W.

DnS. Looked stronger this game, but once again reached a saturated hand (1 card left) after using 8 cards already and, thus, without any remaining offense.

Fax. Took advantage of a Mana surplus from 2x Mana Flare then protected themselves with N-Disk. Easy win that looked closer than it was. 

FD. Mana Flare can be such a beast, but it's like Howling Mine in that it requires recourse to the major gift you give the opponent. Maybe it's M-Kill. Maybe it's M-Freeze. Maybe it's something else, but it's lousy to summon, then while for your opponent to leverage before you do. Hmm

Doom. Fantastic win here for the 4th win in a row!! A crowded battlefield so Darwin's 4 1/1 Cat, Kaysa and a Wolly Spider staring at four 4/4 live artifacts thanks to T-Song. But the game MVP, Tranquility put an end to that, granting the Doom creatures daylight to grab the W. Two Sylvan Library and a Waiting in the Weeds were critical, as was Kaysa. This was a different kind of pressure that Darwin could put on the opponent, utilizing Forest creatures and a team-wide pump. Very cool.

Boom. Tough loss, they had the victory in their sights but couldn't close. A Avoid Fate was in-hand, but it's only good against Instant/Enchantment-types (not Sorcery). Pretty big disadvantage in that sense.

Monsters. Getting Misty, they survive this one, honestly, with a late-Island to enable a Transmute Artifact into Illusionary Mask and, of course, the Dreadnought. Two turns later, it was all over. Good thing, too, as the twin Black Vise on the other end would've closed things out two turns later. They lost 3 Island in this one.

NLFY. First turn Black Vise and little else. They destroyed a few lands, but were stuck on two Swamp nearly the whole game. Hard to win on only two lands. They sat on a Dark Ritual hoping one more swamp would grant them either the Sengir Vampire in-hand, or defense with Hellfire. Never came.

Things. Oh yeah!! They survive a 3x land loss Armageddon to play Living Death combo for the demonstrative win over the #1 seed!! That said, this team is mighty light on creatures somehow. They play a Buried Alive for the SOB, Kraken and Fog Elemental (bc a Kraken was already in the graveyard). Grabbing the Fog Elemental is pretty lackluster in that scenario. Gotta be something else they can resurrect!

Thrulls. Win a no contest.

Fault. Summon several h-kill but otherwise had nothing. That makes it six losses in a row.

X. Skate to victory here, pull a few tricks out of their sleeves with an early Worldly Tutor, a late WoG and a 5/6 Lhurgoyf for the win. 

YCBI. Meekstone slowed 'em down a bit but that was about it.

DnS. DnS. Oh baby! Erase a 4-game losing streak with a big win over 5th-seeded Team 10. A Fork-Incinerate followed a 4/4 Rukh to close things out after some early-DD (Ball Lightning, Chain Lightning, Fireball). Solid win.

Team 10. The W was in sight after a Worldly Tutor had a BoP in wait for a Yawggy next turn, but couldn't get there bc of Wheel of Fortune. Tough loss.

Frottle. Call it a win streak! Two in a row for the Thrulls who lean on 1-2-3 Dark Ritual for an early Lord of the Pit. They benefited from a Kormus Bell as well, activating 4x Swamps into creatures. 

Doom. Ends a 4-game win streak. Tough draw game in which they pull 8 lands and only 6 cards in the 8-turns. Don't know that they could've handled the big guy anyway, but wasn't a game they were going to win.

NLFY. Big division win here as they climb back to 10-8 tired with the Machine. Two big cards in this game were N-Disk, which killed off a 5/5 Flying Flowstoner and then Hellfire which knocked a Tetravus. Nice win.

Fax. Had all the early momentum, putting the Flowstoner out on turn-2. No Landia's defense was too strong though.

FD. Pick up their first win in 5 games!! Big one, too, against a top 4 team. They were unleashed in this one, slinging anything and everything onto the battlefield thanks to 8 Mana and a Mana Flare. Pretty fun.

Monsters. Well that stinks. They weren't able to pull a second Nought after their first one was killed, and even dipped into their secondary strategy a bit, destroying 28 cards in the library thanks largely to Grindstone.

Fault. Lucky win here, they were staring down the barrel of a gun that was Fallow Wurm until a Mind Twist was drawn. They had 3x Megrim on the board already - THREE! The game-saving move saw two opposing cards discarded for 12 points of damage and the W. Phew!

Boom. Didn't look great in this game, but drew well late. They staved off the Fault namely because BAF had a tough mana situation. Felt like a crappy team in this one :-\

Gloves. The GLOVES!!!! Might be the first time in League history that the last place team beats the first place this, especially this far into things!!!! What an execution here. They were patient, resolute and somehow showed as the perfect foil to the now-not-so-indestructible-Potatoes. By the end, it was 2x Howling Mine, 2x U-Dreams and a Teferi's Puzzle Box that blasted their doors off. So great! Might this be the turning point in their season?! We will find out!

Potatoes. Despite facing an opponent with next to no creatures, they still couldn't pull this off. It seemed like they were turning the tide, but some Winds of Change shifted the balance yet again. The Potatoes have now lost an incredible 3 in a row.

Slivers. Looked svelte this game and for their 4th W in a row, Trotted out two dual lands, a City of Brass and two more basics for a Metallic, Muscle, Winged, Victual and Queenie. Weren't going to be stopped by anything but Living Death and it never showed.

Things. Not designed to withstand the early pummeling :-\

Fault. First time I felt like they looked good / cohesive in a long time. Need to swap out Seismic Assault for more M-Kill. Maybe this can still work as is..?

Boom. Looked pretty dreadful,. Not much jump in this team.

Potatoes. End a 3-game skid by winning the rematch against the Gloves. After a turn-2 Disenchant on a U-Dreams, the shifted increasingly in SPs favor. Guess they're pissed off with the #3 seed :)

Gloves. Too bad! They stuck it to SP in the lsat match-up and started aggressively with the U-Dreams in play. Crazy, but I feel like there's still some goodness in this team despite being 2nd to last..

X. Fourth win in a row, starting to look like a real BEAST out there. This was surgical. They had 3x Kird Ape out fast alongside a BoP. An early Wrath of God was vetoed, but a few turns later they summoned a second one to wipe 7 creatures off the board. A Lhurgoyf was played the same turn. A second one came a turn later. 2x Lightning Bolt were used also on offense. Smack the YCBI for 16-damage on the final turn.

YCBI. Put a good defensive front out there with 3x Castle, a Kismet and a Wall of Swords but needed a second Counterspell to stop the Wrath. Third loss in a row.

Drillers. Win the longest game of the season so far. I was ridiculously distracted by Jenny's Tiktok feed so it's possible this game was a fluke.. Anyway, they were able to exhaust the Monsters of all available counters and Dreadnought, of which there were many. 

Monsters. Ran out of defense against the DD. Probably should've attacked their library a bit more.

Machine. Win the rematch and keep their slight lead over their division foe! They thrived on 9 lands in this game, getting a Xanthic Statue out, then a Clockwork Avian and then finally a Flowstone Sculpture. Easy win.

NLFY. Probably one of the few times they come up short despite an early Black Vise. Only drew 3 lands and couldn't keep up.

Things. Win the rematch with great draw in this game. Early Kraken removed from the game gave them a life buffer. SotN with a U-Mu ended the duel.

Sliver. Ends their 4 game win streak :( The early Living Death ruined their chances, not even a Queenie could save them.

DnS. Make it two in a row! Rukh Egg really shined here, being converted into a Rukh twice in this game, with enough leftover firepower to take out a Yawggy and end the game on a Fireball. Nicely done, they looked good here.

Team 10. Didn't put up much of a fight with only 2 lands, but were still able to put out some cards (8). Looked vulnerable to flying creatures.

Doom. Great win for Darwin the new way - leveraging more C-Pump with Overrun winning the game on a Kaysa and F-Elves. Fun to see it play out that way, and Sylvan Library is still the engine that makes this thing run. Really great card.

Thrulls. Had some creatures, had some M-Pump in hand but didn't have solid O or D. Just sort of puttering.

Things. Smackdown! Despite having only two lands in play, they manage to wrangle their team captain and big PK out into battle. Pretty cool to see that.

Gloves. Also only two mana, couldn't get their 2x U-Dreams out there but it didn't much matter. Wil-o-the-Wisp is worthless against this opponent.

X. A very nice, rhythmic win here. Three lands and a BoP gave way to a Djinn, then a second one. Both were matched up with a Wall of Earth, stifling any attack. But a fifth land opened the door to a WoG, then a 6/7 Lhurgoyf the next turn. A Hurricane blasted a Granite Gargoyle, and that was it.

DnS. One more mountain and this commentary might be a bit different. They sat on 4 DD spells - highly irregular for this group.

Doom. A really great win in the finale to hold onto the 6th seed! They lost 5 lands in this game. The Sylvan Library was huge at keeping them afloat, especially the Hurricane draw to kill off a Rathi Dragon.

Fault. Looked like a real team in this game. The Mana kill thing with Ants and Bauble was in full force. They may have win this game without the timely Hurricane.

Machine. Cruise to victory behind solid mana (5x), including a Mishra's Factory, and then a Lotus Petal, a Mana Vault and a Sol Ring. This team consistently trots out a TON of mana. 2x Juggernaut and the Factory went unopposed.

FD. Too much mana and no battlefield defense. They killed off both Juggernaut but were too slow.

NLFY. Man, when this team wants to "control" they can really flip the switch. They started with a Strip Mine, used only 1x M-Kill card, and relied on Cyclopean Tomb to un-Island the Monsters' mana base. Worked insanely well, and the 2x Black Vise capitalized. 

Monsters. One of those games where they sputter, sputter some more and end up cycling thru endless Scroll Rack abilities hoping for the magical Mask-Nought combo. Makes me feel like there's still more roster movement ahead for this crew. They just don't have a prayer when the big guy doesn't show up.

Sliver. Oh baby, win a big time head to head to regain the #2 ranking. They had a nice mana-to-Sliver mix in this one, able to deploy 4x (Crystalline, Muscle, Winged, Victual) to grab the W. Muscle was especially helpful as he pumped up the Crystalline to a 3/3 and bigger than the opposing White Knight.

Potatoes. Looked real pedestrian here, summoning very little opposition outside of a single Armageddon that might've been too late, frankly. Interesting loss for them here.

YCBI. Erase a 3-game losing streak by knocking off Team 10 for a second time. They're really, REALLY well positioned to beat this team. It's just a great match-up for them. Meekstone absolves the Yawggy concern AND the Unholy Strength on a little guy move. Pretty great. Coupled with Wall of Swords, they're just very quick to put up massive defensive front on Team 10.

Team 10. Lose again, it's just not clear how to go on the offensive w/ a creature base against YCBI. Feels like they have to fall on the Winter Orb and Black Vise combo instead of stocking up battlefield.

Boom. No contest, pu activate 3x 4/4 artifacts with the Song.

Frottle. One mana, tough hand. This team is terrible. There's a little too much sacrificing required of their creature base. It leaves the roster unbalanced.

games.commentary: Sliver Lining def. Green Boss in 6 turns — a dominant performance by the Season 7 champions.

narrative.preview: Three years!? It's been three years!?!? Well, there you have it folks.. Season 7 was such an overnight sensation (See: took 2 yrs) that yours truly needed >1,000 days to run it back! Fortunately, the medley of teams on a hella-low simmer for Season 8 are looking saucy as ever; the Slivers are back to defend their first title, NLFYs off-season was eerily quiet, Darwin's pissed off (again) and so are Extinction, Dark n Stormy and Team 10 - all of which remain largely unchanged from last season. Bad Ass Fault is sporting some new fancy H-Kill. YCBI found its groove in the playoffs and is prancing in bright eyed and bushy tailed. The Swamp Things are downright terrifying. Clockwork's nuts and bolts parade worked like clockwork in Season 7 so they're obviously back to cause problems. That makes 10 returners from the obscenely long Season 7 meaning six new entrants take the stage this time around.

teams.writeup: Sorry, Dad. YCBI helped remind me just how abhorrent it is to play a control deck (thanks, Stasis!) and how I'd like to avoid them at all costs if possible. Yeeesh, this was brutal! Most losses were logged as 'No contest,' which tracks, but the late-season improvement suggests a deck still searching for the right shape. The record is brutal. The foundation might not be. There's an elegance to the Vigilance ability and one that'll be explored in more detail in the off-season.

This one hurts.. an O-10 team, these guys just felt flat, stuck in no man's land without a real identity. The black and green hits so good, and I loved finding a way to enable 'Ol Yawggy, but it's just 'too hard' with this roster. Arguably their best angle to do damage was Black Vise - a copycat of NLFY which just felt bad. Ideally each team has unique win conditions and almost no card overlap whatsoever. The most enjoyment I got, outside of seeing Yawggy hit the field, was watching the artifact gameplay around Relic Barrier with Ankh / Orb to mess with opponents.

Another OG heads out to pasture, but hey it just never clicked. Ever since the Rukh Egg farce of Season 4 they've been on a seemingly wayward mission to find themselves. This season's Bottomless Pit addition showed some spunk but they had no obvious complement. Core to the strategy is discarding opponent cards, so maybe graveyard manipulation plays a role? There could be something to explore between Bad Ass Fault and Swamp Things, and explore we shall.

Finally, no more potatoes!! The goddamn poster child for insufferable game play, the Potatoes' 'Land Tax + Armageddon' combo will annoy us no more! Resetting the game with ever-generating tokens is tough sledding, and we reached a breaking point this season, dreaming instead of a mono-white outfit with spunk, style, identity and cohesion without the drawbacks. Alas, a new squad is forming to hit those notes.

Some teams in this league are just boom-or-bust. Clockwork was blanked in the playoffs, 3-0, by Folk Music. They played two well fought games, one at the hands of Energy Flux - literally the only card in the league designed to decimate the gears on this squad - and another because of Maze of Ith. You might say Folk Music was uniquely advantaged against Machine, a fair and accurate assessment given Energy Flux's multiple appearances in the series. This is a funky team, no question, and are still a few revisions away from being a tough out. Rocket Launcher was a fun addition. They'd benefit from some more defense, probably. For now, they're weird, nostalgic and at times pretty powerful. Good enough to take another lap in Season 9.

Gotta hand it to 'em, the Doomers hung in there with the Slivers in Round 1 (3-2), forcing a Game 5 after a gutsy 4 to -9 victory in Game 4. Darwin's season was a bit wonky donkey, cycling 45+ cards to try and find an identity. By seasons end, I think they did it. The Sylvan Library + Call of the Wild + Worldly Tutor + Big World Beaters is super fun and seems to have promise. In retrospect this makes a ton of sense - green employs some big boys, including an all-time favorite in Force of Nature, and I'm excited to see if there's enough of a draw engine around them to be viable and, dare I say it, competitive.

Underworld Dreams continues to vex ye ol Commish. Long established as a foundational card, singing loudest while opponents reload. Not a primary strategy, but maybe not a secondary one either? They wrapped up a 7-15 season, good enough for 15th overall and the #7 seed in the North Conference - a 'not great Bob' conclusion to the regular season, then proceed to stun the 2nd overall team, Mean Machine, by sweeping them in Round 1 (3-0). Round 2 was a different story yet perfectly encapsulated the Shuffler conundrum: Howling Mine really has to be a one-sided benefit. Perhaps U-Dreams can only work if it's (a) complementary, (b) partners with a lock strategy of some kind and (c) likely cannot exceed dual color deck design.

Win their Round 1 series against longtime foe, Team 10 (3-1) in a slight upset before getting humbled in Round 2 by TTFs (3-0). Swamp Things feel very 'boom-or-bust' to me with a game plan highly dependent on the Living Death combo. There are times where Unstable Mutation plays a sizable role, and I think that angle needs to be preserved. I also realized about mid-season that they support 'red' yet don't really use it - this after a few games of namely black/blue hands with too much red mana. It makes sense to test a sans-red version in Season 9. Outside of a sterling 15-5 affair in Season 5, they're 48-52 all-time.

Even Steven over here, the Stormy's are 32-30 across their 3 seasons in The League - but a consistent force in the playoffs. Underseeded against both opponents, Dark N Stormy took it to #4 Extinction in Round 1 (3-2) and #1 NLFY (2-3 in losing Round 2 effort). DnS was arguably the perfect foil for a board-clearing Extinction team with nary a single creature to lose in gameplay. Their speed helped too, taking two games in 5-turns. Against #1 NLFY, the Storm took a 2-1 lead after a 7-turn 3 to -4 down-to-the-wire victory in Game 3. DnS continues to win me over in the nostalgia department. The continuous flow of bolts and flames work wonders aesthetically and at times they are absolutely pulverizing.

'Plucky' is dead right. They ran into a tough opponent in Round 1, losing to Smashed Potatoes in a 3-game sweep. Only once in the series did they put real pressure on the Blancos. The Atts sac strategy ran into a buzzsaw as the Potatoes token brigade came on in full force with Kjeldoran Home Guard + Kjeldoran Outpost pumping out all manner of 1/1 soldiers and knights. Sacrificing a creature to destroy another creature is a compelling tactic - it's similar to what Extinction does, yet doesn't employ a Lhurgoyf equivalent. Hmm...

Lose a WILD Round 1 matchup against old foe; Dark N Stormy (3-2). Took Game 1 & Game 3, one of the few teams equipped to handle - and even benefit - from having their creature base lit on fire. Game 1 took 12-turns, Game 3 took 13. They lost Game 2 and Game 5 in 5-turns each, and Game 4 in 8-turns. Pace played a role in this series but so did the fact that X's main strategy - to wipe all creatures off the board - does next to nothing against DnS! One might say this was the very worst opponent for Extinction to play. One additional note; haste works really well for this team. Lightning Elemental played a larger role than anticipated this season.

A strong, surprising season by the mono-red outfit, but their first round exit to 7th seeded Hand Grenade tells a strange, slightly harrowing tale. Their Game 1 loss notes capture it well: 'Looked slow and clunky here - took awhile to get Gauntlet of Might out and then they couldn't use it (?). Goblin Recruiter feels like the only unlock for this team - I'd rather be sending goblins out every single turn for mass chaos.' The goblins, at least in my mind, should be a wild, reckless outfit with oodles of ammo and overwhelming swarm potential. This team ain't it yet. The Gauntlet of Might is probably not a good card in this deck. It's not yet clear how to better nail the WTF vibes while keeping them competitive, but that's a primary task for the off-season.

Unfortunately for everyone involved, this team is good and they further proved their mettle this post-season. The Music didn't lose a game until the semi-finals, sweeping #7 seeded Clockwork (3-0) in Round 1 then #6 seeded Smashed Potatoes in Round 2 (3-0) before going down in a respectable 7-games to #1 seeded TTFs. It was a series of swings, starting out 2-0 before taking three in a row, then finally dropping the last two. The Music were rhythmic, deafening and mesmerizing at once it seemed. They'll be back next season, surly and spiteful. A hell of a run, and so fun to have yet another very competitive, distinct team in The League.

The defending champ, the Slivers were one of the early favorites to contend for the Season 8 title. They survived a closer-than-expected series against Darwin (3-2) in Round 1 before blitzing Hand Grenade (3-0) in Round 2. The North Division semi-finals had them match up with #1 overall seed, No Lands For You, and that's when things turned south. NLFY ripped off four straight victories in an eye-opening sweep, decimating Colten's spirit in the process - Slivers #1 fan. The Slivers dropped the first two games in 6-turns each and namely due to Black Vise (Game MVP). They wrap Season 8 slightly humbled yet with a few tricks up their sleeves heading into Season 9.

Well, well, hello there #1 overall seed! NLFY pummels #8 YCBI (3-0) in the Round 1 opener before rallying back against #5 Dark N Stormy (3-2) after starting down 2-1. The semi-final sweep of #3 Sliver Lining was all too sweet, propelling NLFY to their fourth finals appearance. This year's finals were scintillating, with TTFs taking a 2-1 lead, then NLFY ripping off two in a row heading into Game 6. TTFs won handily by 21 pts in only 5-turns to force Game 7, and Game 7 came down to the wire. It was a crushing defeat for the all-blacks but they were right there!

It took a single season but in many ways, it's taken a lot longer than that. It took 8 builds to find the right shell for the illustrious Illusionary Mask - Phyrexian Dreadnought combo. Alas, it was worth the wait, as the Trick or Treat Freaks stampeded their way through Season 8 playoffs like a band of sugar-crazed 5th graders ding-dong-ditching a second neighborhood. The playoffs put them to the test, outlasting 8th seeded Bad Ass Fault in Round 1 (3-2), then pummeling Swamp Things in Round 2 (3-0) before taking it to their rival mono-blue outfit, Folk Music, in the Division Final (3-2). They saved their best for last with a 7-game thriller against the #1 overall seeded No Lands For You (4-3), including winning the final two games after being down 3-2. Three cards make this roster work; Scroll Rack, Transmute Artifact & Copy Artifact. They are the draw engine, and it's very unconventional - part of the reason it's so fun. Ironically, their predecessor, Mist Monsters, lost in the finals in Season 7 as a #5-seed. Thus, two seasons in a row the Mask-Nought combo has found postseason success. Congratulations Trick or Treat!

teams.team: Smashed Potatoes + The Circus → Smashed Circus
teams.writeup: Two teams merged mid-season to form Smashed Circus, combining their rosters and combining their records going forward. The merged outfit finished 9-13 overall, reflecting a season of adjustment as the two philosophies were integrated.

Overdrawn and Hand Grenade merged mid-season to form Over Grenade. The combined squad finished 7-15, as the pairing of Underworld Dreams with the artifact lock strategy showed flashes but couldn't find consistent footing.

report_cards.writeup: Sorry, Dad. YCBI helped remind me just how abhorrent it is to play a control deck and how I'd like to avoid them at all costs if possible. Most losses were logged as 'No contest,' which tracks, but the late-season improvement suggests a deck still searching for the right shape.

report_cards.statmuse: Went 2-9 in the 2nd half, worst mark in the league
report_cards.writeup: Bad Ass Fault lived on the edge all season. When the pieces aligned, the deck looked every bit as dangerous as advertised. Consistency was the missing ingredient. The Bottomless Pit + Megrim + The Rack combo is devastating, but how is it best supported?

Overdrawn's premise was simple — shuffle hands until you land the deadly combo and deal damage with Underworld Dreams. They stumbled to a 4-11 record prompting a total shift in construction back to Trick or Treat and Hand Grenade.

Clockwork entered their second season with grand aspirations, however stumbled out the gate losing their first 5 games. This roster requires excessive mana to function, so cards like Lotus Petal, Mana Vault and Urzatron are mission critical.

Strangest season yet for Darwin, yet an important one. They finished the regular season third worst in Damage Dealt per Game. The middling-ness prompted much card shuffling, giving way to a fork in the road for the mono-green OG: build around brute force or build around low-cost creatures.

report_cards.theme: Circus was so bad we had to bring back our least favorite team ever
report_cards.writeup: The Circus was arguably ill-conceived from the start. It's honestly a shock that they lasted 9 games. We did, however, remove the Zuran Orb from the deck, as it was the only way we could stomach play with the mono-white outfit once again.

report_cards.statmuse: Owners of a 5-game win streak + 4-game lose streak!
report_cards.writeup: Swamp Things were the league's ultimate boom-or-bust team. The highs came fast, while the lows arrived just as quickly. They were right around the league average on DGT, yet second-to-last in DTT, leaving little margin when things went sideways.

Team 10 is a fascinating squad, controlling more games than their record suggests — finishing them was the hard part. Not known for intense speed, the team led the league in Deploy-W. The bright spot was tempo control — few teams dictated pace as well.

report_cards.theme: Easy BateMobile, not too fast baby
report_cards.statmuse: Owner of the fastest win (3-turns vs Things) and the slowest (23-turns vs Sliver Lining)
report_cards.writeup: Ah, Dark N Stormy. You bring the nostalgia in droves, old friend! Dark N Stormy's record states the obvious — they either win or they lose and do one or the other roughly 50% of the time.

Extinction really found themselves this season, leaning heavily into Survival of the Fittest to further rally around Lhurgoyf / creature kill strategy. It's important to mention that two of their win streak victories came against Top 3 teams.

Out of the ashes of Thrull Frottle comes the promise of new mainstay; The Attritionists. This revised version, building around Grave Pact, supercharged the sac engine to great effect and fell back to the all-too-familiar might mites strategy with Bad Moon.

MJ was truly onto something! His Merfolk reincarnate deck fared real well, too well honestly, in their inaugural season. Folk Music defaults to mighty mites, buttressed by some of the best defense around and some chicanery.

At long last, the illustrious Mask-Nought combo has a home! Trick or Treat plays on the prior Mist Monsters configuration, surrounding the game-ending combo with tutors, draw advantage and counter defense.

report_cards.theme: The Pulis Avenue rims of The League
report_cards.statmuse: 3.22 DGT; only team 3+ in this category
report_cards.writeup: Man, where to begin. The Slivers, the defending champs, felt like the team to beat all season long. There wasn't a single game where they felt like the underdog. They were fast to win, resilient when they lost, high-impact on each turn and minimalist in deployment.

report_cards.theme: Bazinga! Goblins are here to stay!
report_cards.writeup: Mean Machine!! The all-reds spent the season doing exactly what they said they would do — and daring opponents to stop it. The identity was never clearer than Goblin Recruiter, who won six Game MVPs.
No Landia, you dog, you!!! The defending stalwart claimed their first ever #1 overall seed behind a stout 1.10 Damage Taken per Turn — comfortably the league's best defensive mark of the season.

game_commentary.commentary:
Potatoes. Man, what a drag-em-out victory this was.. 17 grueling turns of tokens battling tokens. The first strike abilities on Order of Leitbur + White Knight made a huge difference in this game. So did Kjeldoran Outpost, but removing Fallen Angel from the game was huge.

Atts. Man, the sac engine was in full effect and was super fun. They sac’ed a lot of lands early which crushed them later on in the deployment department. Grave Pact, where were you!?! Also, against almost anyone else the Jinxed Idol plays a huge role, but the Potatoes were pumping out tokens faster than the Atts - so nullified.

TTFs. Well, this team inches nearer to “mainstay” status with another win - this time skirting an early Hymn to Tourach to pull Transmute Artifact -> Mask-Nought combo. Really fun when it works.

BAF. Right there, maybe need to focus mostly on H-Kill against this opponent (vs M-Kill).

Hand Grenade. So this is the best recent example of what this team should be — 2x Badlands, 1x Mountain + 1x City of Brass send a late U-Dreams, Teferi’s Puzzle Box and Winds of Change onto the field for a big chunk of damage - then 2x Lightning Bolt do some more. Pretty good!

Mean Machine. Looked slow and clunky here - took awhile to get Gauntlet of Might out and then they couldn’t use it (?). Goblin Recruiter feels like the only unlock for this team — I’d rather be sending goblins out every single turn for mass chaos.

X. Wow, what a game! This one comes down to the wire w/ X dispatching a critical Furnace of Rath to save the game, then winning on the next turn with Carnassid + LIghtning Elemental. Extremely fun game. X lost 7x creatures in this game (!) yet had the mana to hold on and win the old fashioned way.

DnS. Man, right there! Sat on a bevy of DD spells and 2x Furnace of Rath in-hand, yet the first was dispatched - nullifying their chance to win. Super fun game.

Sliver. Largely unchallenged in this game,as most of the damage against them was done by their own 3x City of Brass!! 

Doom. Something to be learned from in this game, as they hit a fork in the road early; destroy lands w/ Kudzu or gain draw advantage with Sylvan Library. It feels like this group wants to do the latter, and that Kudzu would pair better in the Emerald Army deck construct where there’s a surplus of mana. Doom should have 4x Worldly Tutor + 4x Sylvan Library at a minimum, maybe with some life gain to offset additional draws.

Machine. So fun! Used the Goblin Recruiter to pull every able bodied Goblin to the top of the library. Kind of a wild move that paid off. Most of the horde was dispatched w/ flamethrowers but they hung in there for the W.

DnS. Not often this team gets out-deployed but they used up everything weapon in the arsenal to kill off as many Goblins as possible. Just didn't  have enough C-Defense to hang in there. Found they would've benefited from more Two-Headed Giants in this one.

Things. The Things were left for dead, let’s be honest, and, yet, drew the only card in the entire deck that could have won them the game. There was, exactly, a 4.44% chance of this happening - we calculated it after the game - what a victory for the Swamp Things to claim a 1-0 lead in the series.

T-10. Dumbfounded! Cannot believe it, had this one wrapped up like a Xmas present and were just plain unlucky, having all but locked out the Things thanks to Winter Orb + 2x Ankh of Mishra + a beefed up Stone-throwing Devils. Ridiculous!!!!

Music. Man, what a duel! It took 25-turns, but the Music outlasted a late 16x Rocket Launcher to win a turn later. Summoning 2x Energy Flux early stifled the Machine, as did 3x Maze of Ith. The Phantasmal Terrain + Lord of Atlantis were critical to overcome Machine defense, but neither showed up til late.

Machine. Used Mindless Automaton well to draw their way to a ridiculous record-setting mana pool (32) but it didn't matter - they couldn't support enough creatures to overcome 3x Maze of Ith and 2x Energy Flux. Brutal. Rocket Launcher was their only chance, and the first one was countered.

NLFY. Textbook win. Turn-1 Black Vise, turn-2 Demonic Tutor for Cyclopean Tomb, turn-3 Dark Ritual -> Tomb, controlled the board.

YCBI. Only drew 2x lands in this game, can’t do that and win against NLFY.

Music. Maze of Ith is such a good card, nullifying Su-Chi and giving the Music makers time to assemble an army. There was some land manipulation here too, with 2x Psychic Venom and a Phantasmal Terrain completely f’ing with the opponent.

Fav. Psychic Venom completely screwed up their gameplay. They were gushing with mana thanks to 2x Mana Vault, but were getting dinged handsomely every single turn. Tough sledding!

Team 10. Damn they looked good here, 2x Black Vise, Relic Barrier + Winter Orb, Mishra’s Factory — helped that Things had no mana synergy, all black hand with no swamp producers.

Things. Reminded me why we’re thinking about removing red from the equation - losing due to mana synergy stinks.

DnS. Goddamn!!!! In 5-turns, the mono-reds throw some SMOKE in what is one of their defining games of the season! A whopping 7x cards deployed for 22x damage. Extremely fun, tie the series.

X. Nothing going in this game thanks to mana mismatch. Makes me wonder how much better they’d be with more multi-mana producers.

BAF. Very nice control game from Fault. They destroyed 3-lands early, then slowly chipped away with 2x Abyssal Specter + 1x Megrim.

TTFs. So funny, they lost the 3x lands though they easily could’ve won with an I-Mask, 2x Copy Artifact and a Transmute Artifact in-hand. If they don’t lose mana early they probably win this game.

NLFY. Turn-6 N-Disk wipes the board clean of a possible YCBI “lock” with Meekstone + Castle. They sat on a Wall of Swords also, so nullifying any Sengir or Demonic Horde attacks. After the board wipe, it was smooth sailing via 2x Order and 1x Sengir.

YCBI. Had some early momentum but couldn’t rebound after the board clear.

DD. Awesome finale to see Y-Ants come out w/ Overrun to power two Elvish Rangers to victory. The Gaea's Touch was critical too in putting it all together. Very fun onslaught to end the game.

YCBI. Were a little stuck on mana in this game, packed away with only three 1/1 Falcons for most of the game.

Slivers. No contest victory.

Doom. Looked super slow in this one, unable to play anything other than a Woolly Spider.

Doom. Darwin lucked out here, you might say, because SL had significant mana issues. The delay for SL gave DD some time to get the trains on the tracks, and their 2x Spider, Djinn and Ants took advantage w/ a swift execution.

SL. Only drew 2x lands in 15x cards.

X. Real gusty win, and the kind you need to pull out to go on a big run. They had a subtle lead mid-game and cleared the deck w/ a Cataclsym. They basically won the restart thanks to Survival of the Fittest and the speed it generated beside additional mana.

DnS. Felt like a game they should’ve won. Didn’t quite slip thru their fingers but in my extremely fatigued state I am thinking I missed a play in there somewhere.

BAF. Holy Pulito!!!!! The Fault drop a Shocker -> Megrim -> Megrim for 30x damage in two turns. Gadzooks, we’ve got a series!

TTFs. Ut oh! They had all their combo cards early but the Shocker forced a re-draw and, I mean goddamn, that was it!

Music. What a slog of a game thanks to Energy Flux (MVP) and Maze of Ith! 17-turns for the Music to effectively wait out the Machine - it was Lord of Atlantis and Voldalian War Machine that did most of the damage.

Machine. Tough way to end the season as literally no other team has a card like Energy Flux. They likely win this game without it.

Things. Swift execution, turn-3 Hidden Horror enables a turn-4 Spirit of the Night w/ 2x Unstable Mutation to follow. 

Team 10. Had a nice early run w/ Mirri + Unholy Strength and may have made this a game without the big guy out there.

Extinction. Late Forest propels the Ehrnam Djinn + Giant Growth rally, then a Bolt + Earthquake came out the following turn for more help to put the Drawers away.

Overdrawn. Super fun, but slow. They couldn't used a counter to save their 'Nought and/or Rathi from being killed off.

Doom. Awesome game for DD — Sylvan Library enables mana ramp and an eventual Force of Nature to completely decimate the Sliver horde! They force a Game 5 finale in the process, extremely fun.

Sliver. Got humbled here, recognizing they could get outgunned against big beefy types if the game goes on long enough - and this one did!

Things. The first team to advance into the Quarterfinals, the Swamp Things have done it! A 13/13 Nicol Bolas graced the ‘field thanks to 2x Volcanic Island and 2x Unstable Mutation, and Team 10 had no answers for the winged hell raiser! Let’s go Things!

Team 10. I wrote before the game, “This is a potential loser leaves town match” and, lo and behold, this may be the end of Team 10 for a while. They played 7x cards and couldn’t gain advantage.. a Winter Orb was countered early which could have changed things. Here’s to looking at you, 10!

TTFs. HUGE win to stay alive in the series and tie it up at 2-2. They withstood a Shocker / Megrim combo to get their own deadly combo out and steal the Shocker in the process. A Counterspell on a nearly game-winning Mind Twist was huge as well - saved 10x damage.

Fault. Look good suddenly, generating a good deal of confusion from the Commish!! Series is 2-2 going back to TTFs home field. Next game wins!

Sliver. Lookout, the Slivers are out to defend their title! NLFY sat like ducks as the Slivers shrugged at Cyclopean Tomb’s wizardry en route to 7x beasts hitting the battlefield for a reckoning. T-5 saw 12x damage done thanks to a Heart Sliver, and then another 15x damage came a turn later. Three dual lands were drawn which is SICK.

NLFY. Worked real hard (8-deployed) for little return. An early Dark Ritual+Demonic Tutor pulled out the Tomb, which was a strong move but it wasn’t enough to fend off the horde. In hindsight, this group should always look for the Nevinyrral’s Disk agains the Slivers. Only way to truly protect themselves from an onslaught.

DnS. Nice win and stay alive in the series, evening at two apiece. Steady Eddy in this game, using DD wisely and creature defense wisely as well (Pyrokinesis, Pyroclasm).

X. Ton of lands in this game, needed to draw more creatures.

Attritionists. So fun!! The early Hidden Horror set the table nicely for offense, and then the next-turn Jinxed Idol-Sengir Aristocrat was beautiful. Grave Pact was drawn at one point and just pairs so nicely with Idol - those two could make music together! 

Folk Music. There’s something fun in here, really enjoyed seeing the revised Lord of Atlantis hit the ‘field. Gonna be fun to figure out how to play with them.

Living. Incredible win and true to form, they put 6x Slivers out there w/ 2x Queen + Coat of Arms in-hand ready to accelerate into “woefully uncomfortable” territory! W/ the W, they advance to the 2nd Round in a “loser goes home” matchup with Darwin. Great win! Their march to defend their title continues!

Doom. Honestly a great effort by Doom. Fog plays a HUGE role, as does Ants. Otherwise, little slow and clunky!! Some good bones in here to work with but it’s not yet clear how it all comes together. That said, took 2 games off the defending champ and took the finale down to the wire.

TTFs. When the combo hits, lookout! Scroll Rack turn-2 enables swift searching for the deadly 3-mana combo. A Transmute Artifact brought another ‘Nought out for good measure. What’s great about this team is the low-CMC of the roster, they could deploy quickly on only a few lands with cards in the 1-2 CMC range. Really hard to keep up when they draw so well from the start.

BAF. Tough way to go out, getting pummeled due to mana inconsistencies. They drew 11x cards in this game, one was a Mountain, one artifact and the rest black cards. That’s fixable, if we’re up for it.

DnS. Oh baby, it’s getting Dark n Stormy out there! The smoke-makers throw 7x DD cards into play for an absolute hell storm, closing the doors on ‘Ol X in only 5-turns. What a win, and a favorable matchup at that.

X. Tough matchup, start out somewhat slow (?) and Drew several dead cards in-hand (2x Wrath, Cataclsym) against a creature-less opponent - only one in the league built this way! Tough luck, but a solid season for X as they finally find themselves in rallying around Survival of the Fittest. See ya, X!

Fault. Slow out the gate w/ mana issues but 2x Megrim + Mind Twist was good for 12-damage to shut the Circus down. Not a single creature made it out for them which is pretty odd but this time it didn’t matter.

Circus. Looked atrocious in this game, but didn’t have much of a chance to play anything (2-deploys) so this ends up being an “incomplete.”

Freaks. Skate by thanks some three timely counters; 2x Living Dead + Necromancy. Otherwise, the Things are sauntering to their next opponent without a worry in the world. Two Flying Men and a Clone dealt all the damage - not too menacing. 

Things. No offense with all the counters. Needed some low-CMC creatures in their to complement the graveyard resurrection strategy.

Team 10. Welcome back, Team 10!!! They’re down 14-1 on the 10th turn, summon ‘Ol Yawggy and immediately storm back to take the victory! Yawggy, Mishra’s Factory and Mirri, Cat Warrior go on to do 18-damage on the next two turns. HELL of a comeback. 

Facts. Well they left the W right on the table. A TON of mana (Sol Ring, Lotus Petal, double-Urzatron and more), but lost Colossus of Sardia and 2x Su-Chi. Probably misplayed the Sardia.

DnS. The storm continues to build! Dealing 22x damage on 3-lands is nothing to sneeze at!! Use 8x DD spells in this one for the largely uncontested victory - important to note their low-MCC profile enables them to dodge the turn-1 Black Vise.

NLFY. Had an ideal first hand w/ Black Vise and 2x Sinkhole but no offense beyond that. Their final hand featured 3x Dark Ritual, 2x Terror + Hellfire except there was nothing to summon and no creatures to kill!!!!

TTFs. Awesome game that saw their combo out on turn-3 thanks to Transmute Artifact. My associate, Colten, pointed out that Swamp Things had been misplayed so this game is officially in dispute!!!!! Quite astute by my associate, I must say!!

Things. Played a Wil-o-the-Wisp + Unstable Mutation vs holding back w/ Counterspell which would have potentially saved them the game. We shall adjust our strategy heading into Game 2!

Music. Drew 3x Lord of Atlantis in this game without much defense from the Potatoes - the Music skate the victory!

Potatoes. Heavy M-Kill combo but were too late to gain advantage - the Lords were already out.

Sliver. Won easily, several mutli-mana to put 8x baddies onto the battlefield. No contest.

HG. Tough matchup, their draw engine strategy kind of strengthens this opponent. Not looking good...

Music. Tidal Warrior goddamn! Barely knew this card was in the deck and, yet, it enables 2x Vodalian Knight and Seasinger to steal Serra Angel for the victory. Sweet win.

Potatoes. It’s official, the land destruction / land tax strategy is completely gone after this playoffs. Enough already.

NLFY. Well, that was a clinic right there. They fall down 20-10 early then gain total control of the board with land kill and Cyclopean Tomb. The latter was an absolute beast, flipping 2x Mountain to neutralize any DnS deployment. Nice win.

DnS. Threw some rain early and likely cruise to victory w/o the mana disruption!

TTFs. Wild finish here, facing a hefty opposing graveyard, they draw'n'play Scroll Rack on turn-8, then Transmute it for Mask into 2x Nought. A turn prior they countered a Living Death. 

Things. Were one turn from winning this game, opting to play a second Hidden Horror instead of keeping mana open for Counterspell defense - oh well! The Counterspell could've been used on the Transmute and potentially saved the game. Right there!

Music. Well goddamn, this one really went the distance - a full 17-turns to find a winner. This was army against army, and the Merfolk had some subtle tactical abilities that won them if game - Voldalian War Machine kept the Potatoes bigger blokes at bay, while Voldalian Knight emerged to team up with Tidal Warrior for the KO. Onto Round 3 they go, matching up with fellow All-Blues; Trick or Treat.

Potatoes. Ran out of gas here with very little defense against opposing creatures. Thanks for the laughs and the lassitude, SP!

Slivers. This team man.. They deal 35x on the final turn to close out the series in a “sweep, sweep, sweep”!! Really marched through Hand Grenade unopposed for the most part, far too fast and.. compounding? They didn’t face opposing swarm and they didn’t have to deal with resources depletion. Easy ride in Round 2.

Hand Grenade. Howling Mine really has to be a one-sided benefit - they powered Sliver ramp and still couldn’t get their 2x U-Dreams out into play. Problem is, that unraveling strategy is real slow. They’re essentially pushing for a lock without much defense. Hmmm….

NLFY. Textbook — destroy 5x lands en route to an easy victory, take a 3-2 series lead.

DNS. What can we say, it was “no lands for them”

Machine. It’s a win streak!! 2x early Goblin Grenade were pretty devastating then later on 2x Bolt and the Balloon Brigade pretty much did the trick. Fun squad so far. Wonder how they could be amplified with artifacts. A Coat of Arms or two would be nice.

NLFY. A t-1 Black Vise, a mid-game Vampire, then 2x Black Knight and it still wasn’t enough. Honestly I’m still scratching my head.. were they too slow? Lacking defense? What? I’m honestly not sure.

Sliver. Boy did they draw well in this game.. 2x Disenchant + 1x Sword to Plowshare to handle 3x Noughts? Wow, fortuitous I’d say!! The multiple dual lands out at one time is just so sexy 😆 

Overdrawn. “They need more creatures” was what i left thinking this game. They can’t just sit there and wait. Maybe some walls do the trick?

Doom. Make it two in a row! Doom get the W thanks to Sylvan Library, picking and choosing their way to mana and Y-Ants to put the pressure on. A late Hurricane sealed the win. Can’t tell if they won this won or Extinction lost it?

Extinction. Not much spice in this one. Had 2x Birds of Paradise and tutor’ed their way to a Djinn but they were pretty lackluster on offense. Come to think of it.. neither team really threw much firepower into the ring this time which is strange considering they’re both “creature” outfits.

DnS. Looked real sharp in this second game, deploying quickly w/ 2x damage thanks to MVP Furnace of Rath. They deployed a Ball Lightning, had another in hand and a Two-Headed G also. Stout!

YCBI. Not a game they're designed to win; too slow, big on creature defense against a creature-less team.

Folk. Oh shit! See the Lord of Atlantis come out alongside Phantasmal Terrain en route to 7x damage and was like, "Damnnn, they're good!" Think they'll be a fun wrinkle here to say based on that game.

Circus. "Not a real team" was the feeling around turn-4 - their last one by the way! Not sure what this is supposed to be but it's only game 2!!

Things. Well, that was easy! Spirit of the Night chewed up the Ants like a handful of peanuts. No contest.

Ants. Either Spirit of the Night is arguably the worst card they could've come up against or I don't know how to leverage this roster yet.. or both!!!

Fault. Bottomless Pit!!! Wow what an addition - saved them from a Black Vise and paired so wonderfully with The Rack and Megrim. Just a terrifying trio and makes me wonder if there should be even more H-Kill in this deck vs maybe M-Kill. For the first time in a while, Fault looked great.

T-10. The Ankh-Vise-Orb combo is deadly but they slowed down in this game partly because of mana shortage. There was also a big miss w/ Yawggy getting discarded right after mana producers but otherwise they were ineffective on offense early on and then got caught in the Bottomless Pit buzzsaw. Team might want Hyppy’s too?

Freaks. Not much resistance once they took care of the Flowstone Sculpture. They march to 2-0 on the back of an Air Elemental + Unstable Mutation. 

Machine. Needed some 3-4 CMC creatures to put some pressure on the Freaks. Problem was, the Freaks were sitting on a Maze of Ith (and had another in hand).

TTFs. I mean... Drew 2x Nought to start + Scroll Rack, so Scroll Rack -> Mask -> 2x Nought -> Transmute Artifact -> 3rd Nought. Game over.

Folk Music. Speed, timely counters and Maze of Ith - that's the only way to win this series.

NLFY. This is textbook stuff from NLFY, sending 2x Vise + 3x land destruction cards to pulverize Slivers. Game1, all blacks.

Slivers. This is the “worst case” outcome for the Slivers, rendered useless by NLFYs imposing defenses.

TTFs. Gah-lee! Similar story as Game 1; Drew Scroll Rack + Nought + T-Artifact. Rack -> Mask + Nought -> Nought. Unreal.

Folk Music. Had the Maze and countered the first Mask but they're gonna need more than that!!!!

Music. Nice win to stay in this thing. Vodalian Mage prevented a Control Magic that would’ve changed the game - sitting on 2x Dandan.

TTFs. Probably win this game without Vodalian Mage.

Music. Tie up the series thanks to Force Spike (Scroll Rack) and Dandan. Sat on another counter and Seasinger too.

TTFs. Had 2x Nought without any draw engines.

NLFY. Man, what a game by the O-10 All-Blacks! Turn-1 Dark Ritual for Sinkhole + Black Vise gave them the early advantage. By turn-6, Slivers were already in a 20-5 hole! More defense came later on with 1x Icequake + 2x Terror and the threat of a Nevinyrral’s Disk. Huge, methodical win to advance to the finals!

Slivers. Tough way to go out, they were completely handled in this game, locked out with minimal lands for the first chunk of the game.

Music. Maybe a season defining win, countering a turn-2 Mask, stealing a Nought on turn-6 for the win.

TTFs. They have four threats Music has figured out that they can sit on Maze, counters and Seasinger to gut out victories. Pretty fun

TTFs. Oh baby! What a series, win the first two, then drop 3-straight only to tie it up and win Game 7 in historic fashion on a mere 4-turns. They drew real well from the start with a Nought + Copy Artifact + Mask - game winner in the opening hand, drew the second land on turn-2 and the path to victory was cleared. Hell of a way to stamp their first season in The League, heading to the ‘ship.

Folk Music. Valiant effort and no shame going down to arguably the most ferocious combination in the league. They put their stamp on things in what is only their first season as a club. The Music were rhythmic, deafening and mesmerizing at once it seemed. Well done, Folks.

Machine. Heyyyyyyyyyy Machine! Cmon now! A turn-6 win is nothing to sneeze at! Mountainwalk won them the game, no question. They were able to take shot after shot without recourse. I find I want this team to do something else in addition to amassing the horde. Not sure yet though..

Overdrawn. Still felt slow and desperate to me. Finally got a Nought + Blood Lust out there for 16x damage but it was too late.

Sliver. WOW by the skin of their mangled teeth they win this one. It was a Winged Sliver that took any C-Defense off the table for DnS - and they were sitting on ample D; Balduvian Horde, Two-Headed G + 2x Wall of Earth. The box score makes this appear lopsided but in reality a last-turn Coat of Arms totally distorted things w/ a whopping 23-damage to claim the W.

DnS. Possibly made a critical mistake using two Bolt on C-Defense instead of DD. They were stuck w/ only 3x mana yet the 3x Howling Mine supplied them w/ ample firepower to make hay. Tough L.

Freaks. Commanding victory w/ a turn-2 Serendib Efreet with a Clone to follow and finally a Control Magic-Dardan. Game over, everyone! Looked solid.

Music. No mana stunted any activity whatsoever. They’re calling this a mulligan victory for their opponents in blue.

Fault. An interesting victory for the Fault who sat on a nice combination of Bottomless Pit-The Rack-Megrim to deal damage no matter what the opposing hand looked like. It took a while, yet their 2x Assassin were ready defensively. A lot of early M-Kill and a 5-card Mind Twist totally disabled the Machine and they never really recovered.

Machine. Ended up seeing 12 lands come through their hand, with 5x in the graveyard early on. The Mind Twist, specifically, really knocked them out given the handful of attackers that hit the gallows; Xanthic Statue, Tetravus, Clockwork Avian, Flowstone Sculpture. Tough loss!

Team 10. Really interesting game, but they held in there and some really useful cards to win the game. Unholy Strength turned a Wormwood Treefolk into a devastating 6/5 force. Winter Orb also held the opponent back, especially the Hecatomb.

Ants Marching. Had some early momentum with Hidden Horror and a Hecatomb, but were stunted by the Winter Orb and never really recovered. Colten, “Some really bad players, and kind of didn’t deal any damage to Team 10 which made Team 10 win the game.”

Circus. The Fallow Wurm + Concordant Crossroads worked out well this time, w/ 2x Wurm moving quickly to dispatch the previously tough Things team.

Things. Faltered out of the gate in this one, needed one early Swamp to access their graveyard but were unable to do so thanks to Stone Rain. Tough start for the Things.

NLFY. Destroy 3x lands en route to easy victory. The ramp of early destruction -> 4x Knight was smooth albeit uncontested. 

TTFs. Sat on the winning combo w/ Mask + Copy Artifact + Transmute Artifact but couldn’t get there fast enough.

TTFs. They force a Game 7!!! What a series this has turned out to be -- this time starting w/ 3x I-Mask + 2x Island, then draw the mighty Nought. Game was over two turns later.

NLFY. Unlucky, just unlucky. Killed off 2x lands on turn-2, but it wasn't enough.

TTFs. Whoa nelly!!!!! The Mask-Nought combo wins the 'ship in a fantastic 7-game series against O-10 team; NLFY!!! This was one helluva finale, getting down to 6-points before going on the offensive. Scroll Rack was integral, shuffling hands turn-after-turn to find more mana, as 3x lands were destroyed early. A turn-1 Island -> Sol Ring -> Scroll Rack gave them a fighting chance, and really this was about buying time to find the combo, which they did via Transmute Artifact and 2x Control Magic to steal 2x Vampire. Really fun game and finale.

NLFY. Damn, losing 2 of 3 to drop the championship is tough and boy were they right there in the end. Black Vise + land destruction absolutely decimated TTFs early on and they were unlucky to hit not one but 2x Control Magic to lose their Vampire. Unreal. Nonetheless, a terrific season by an old stalwart.

Marked as resolved

Re-opened

Slivers. Throwing 4x dual-lands and a City of Brass meant they could summon anything in their bag, and they did! Two Sliver Queen waltzed onto the battlefield unobstructed after 2x Metallic, a Muscle and a Crystalline did some early damage to win the battle of the unbeatens; #1 & #2 in Week 3. 

Machine. It’s possible the Machine could’ve mounted an offensive w/o the Queen. There’s still work to do with this squad but the potential is there, it feels like!

NLFY. Take the W thanks to 2x Dark Ritual and 3x M-Kill cards. One of the Dark Ritual put a 7/7 Entropic Specter on the battlefield and he went on the offensive completely unguarded. NLFY looked real good in this one. Colten comments; “No Lands killed X because X only played 2 cards and NLFY 3x lands yet still won it with Entropic Specter.

X. Had no juice in this one after they lost their initial swath of lands - they just couldn’t play anything and sat idly by as a result. Colten comments; “X had a tough game, did not hang in there at all, had no juice just like Dad said, but they gave it all they got.” Tommy comments; “X couldn’t play anything, ran out of cards to play with no lands and lost by 15 pts. They tried their best to play the game.. they tried but they just couldn’t do it.”

Overdrawn. It’s a wild team.. they sat somewhat idly waiting for the right combination of cards, used some counters to protect the Mask-Nought combo and it was game over. It’d be interesting if there was a benefit to cycling cards like this somehow. Maybe they need Wisp’s in there too? Hard to say.

YCBI. Pecked away to move within striking distance and were close to putting an Archangel into play as well. They’re solid.

DnS. Kind of sang in this game honestly. A Furnace of Rath put Stormy on the offensive as Pyroclasm wiped out 4x creatures, including a Yavimaya Ants, to clear the way for Ball Lightning, Lightning Bolt and Shock for 22 damage. Bazinga!

Doom. Looked good honestly, using 2x Sylvan Library to find Forest for an eventual Living Lands and possible Waiting in the Weeds. They were right there.

Folk. Wow, they look good in this game! Dandan + Tidal Warrior is a great combo, then the Lord of Atlantis w/ the C-Pump… yeah baby! Energy Flux is a death knell for Facts Machine - like, not many cards out there in the league that are as formidable. Sweet victory and naked lap here.

Fax. Looked a little slow and clunky but would’ve been a slightly different story if they didn’t have 4x mana locked up to Energy Flux each turn. Not dead yet I don’t think.

Freaks. Was dicey for a minute before they hit 3x lands to march 3x Serendib Efreet for the W. They were sweating for a minute but just drew more favorably than T-10.

T-10. First-turn Sol Ring -> Felwar Stone -> Black Vise was sick.. and then nothing. They put 2x Stone and a Winter Orb out there with nothing else to play. Next time!

Fault. Looked stunning in this game w/ a turn-2 Shocker -> Megrim for 11-damage on turn-4. Devastating combo, those two. Hit it again the next turn for 7x followed by a Hymn To Tourach for the W. Wow.

Things. First-turn Dark Ritual -> Buried Alive w/ the mana and 2x Necromancy + Living Death in the hand. Game was over if not for Shocker forcing them to discard and re-draw. Very fun game and tough loss for the Things.

Ants. Hell yeah! Now we're talkin! Ants swap out 14 cards in Week 4 ahead of their matchup w/ The Circus and suddenly look like a real squad! Turn-1 Carnophage put some early heat on, but it was a turn-2 Dark Ritual -> Hidden Horror that really fired things up! Two Diabolic Edict nullified Circus defense and that was all she wrote - in FIVE turns AND ON TWO FUCKIN MANA!!! That's 6x deploys on 2x mana! We might have a squad here! LETS GO ANTS!

Circus. Clearly not a real team vs a mashup of cards. Unclear what the path forward is, but it sure as hell ain't this!!!!

Green Boss. Good golly miss mossy molly! Only 3x mana, but a bunch’a elves vault a 20/20 Primalcrux onto the field alongside a 7’6 Troll, Living Hive and Cavalier of Thorns in only 4-turns. Yikes.

TTFs. Strong initial draw w/o any path to the Mask.

TTFs. Damn!! Initial hand was a beast, Mask, 2x Nought, Copy Artifact, Sol Ring and some lands. Turn-1 Sol Ring -> Mask, Turn-2 3x Mask, Turn-4 game over!

Red Boss. Not nearly as synergistic as GREEN. May require some tinkering.

Black Boss. A couple’a bone-men deal the lion’s share of the damage as Black Boss moves through TTF like a warm knife through butter.

TTFs. Too much mana despite sitting on Mask + Transmute Artifact. There was nothing else to summon.

Machine. Easy win due to land trouble at X HQ, but instructive on how many MM games may go.. Goblin Recruiter pulls out a host of Goblin King + Raging Goblin. If that’s the case, what else would you want around them to preserve the move? This is precisely how they won this game though, again, it was largely uncontested. 

X. Struggle with mana deployment though sat on three board clearers that would’ve been essential to defending the Goblin horde. Next time!

YCBI. Wow, with Stasis in this crew they really are a game-changer. They just took down an undefeated Sliver team and while Soraya the Falconer got the MVP it was clear Stasis completely locked the Sliver from being able to retaliate after a big mana deploy + attack. Soraya & Castle pumped up 3x Falcon to attack and hold off any Slivers on D. Vigilance really played a role here, too, now that I think about it. Win #1 for YCBI but DAMN. Lookout, league!

Slivers. Tough loss, started w/ a clunky hand and took a swing at Sliver Queen via Worldly Tutor immediately. Not sure if it worked or not but they just felt a little slow believe it or not.

NLFY. Solid win, exercised total control over DnS mana resources with a combination of 3x M-Kill cards, 1x Domenic Hordes and 1x Cyclopean Tomb. Once all DnS defenses had been dispatched via 2x recently added Terror - the Hordes could lob 5x damage grenades at will while Black Vise chipped away. Also worth underlining the 16x cards deployed in this one - damn! Pretty solid.

DnS. Drew a ton of mana but couldn't make use of it early on with an odd start of cards; Giant, Fork, Pyroclasm, Manabarbs, Pyrokinesis. They just didn't draw enough L-Kill in this game.

Doom. AWESOME win by the Doom! They came out the gate real strong in a way very unique to their team; Turn-2 saw 8x mana on the board thanks to 3x Forest, 1x Gaea's Touch, Sol Ring, Llanowar Elves. Real cool - used it to put a Ants on the board who proceeded to do 15x damage. One game-winning Hurricane was countered, but a fortuitous draw via Teferi's Puzzle Box brought another which was huge considering they were staring down the barrel of a 10/4 Tresserhorner!

Overdrawn. Looked way better than in the past; Lord of Tresserhorn is a wonderful fit in many ways, Counterspell made a huge difference and Transmute Artifact would've brought out a 'Nought on the next turn. Synergies running wild, very fun.

Folk Music. I mean, this team is real good. To stunt a 5/3 First Strike while attacking undefended via Islandwalk.. they just smacked Team 10. There was even a Winter Orb in play! Voldalian War Machine is an incredible card and, paired with Lord of Atlantis, quickly becomes a total monster; 4/7 in this game.

Team 10. Deployed 8x cards and it just didn't turn into much. They had a Devils w/ 2x Unholy Strength, a Winter Orb / Relic Barrier combo, 2x Mishra's Factory and still couldn't get much done. Kind of a wild, instructive game for them honestly. Lot of work done for little return.

Things. Turn-2 Lim Dul's Vault enabled a Buried Alive for 3x life - great trade. That paved the way for a Dark Ritual-Living Dead, ending the game two turns later.

Facts. Came out strong w/ Mana Vault + Juggernaut, had more mana to play and more creatures (2x Su-Chi + Colossus) so in a bit longer of a game they take this one.

Freaks. Skate by in this one thanks to an incompetent opponent. They're not quite right yet (Maze of Ith?) but there's good bones in here to work with - not to mention they're the only remaining undefeated team!!! 

Circus. Think we need to start over here.. maybe Smashed Potatoes comes back? This squad ain't it.

Ants. Goddamn, we got another one! Grave Pact + Fallen Angel + Sengir Autocrat + Lake of the Dead just turned a 3x damage turn into a 11x damage turn to win the game. That was awesome, clever, synergistic and super fun! Let's go Ants!

Fault. 4-game win streak is over! Looked real slow and uncoordinated in this one. Megrim hit the board + Shocker + The Rack + Mind Twist in hand but they needed more speed. Ants were down to 5 on the 9th turn - that's a lot of time in a slow drain w/ 9x lands hitting the graveyard for Ants. Tough one actually.

Machine. Okay, this is a really important one.. Mean Machine deploy 9x cards on 2x mana. That's unusual, but they also played basically three separate games in this one; (1) early attack w/ 2x Raging Goblin, (2) post-God of War attack w/ 1x Raging Goblin, then (3) a post-Serra Angel Goblin Grenade for the win. Not many teams could've pulled that off. Pretty cool versatility here.

YCBI. Man Stasis is annoying lol keeping track of tapped vs past-tapped resources, but hey it works real well w/ a vigilant squad. Tough loss despite a ton of mana.

Sliver. Turn-1 Worldly Tutor brought in a Muscle Sliver to give an immediate edge / speed to the 2x Winged Sliver in hand. The Gemstone Mine, however, is what unlocked the flying foes! A late Coat of Arms unleashed hell with a 24x damage turn for a demonstrative victory.

Doom. Looked a bit slow here with a 1x land draw in the initial hand. Tough luck, though they were close to unleashing the Sylvan Library - Call of the Wild combo.

NLFY. Really balanced victory killing 2x lands and discarding 4x cards w/ Hymn to Tourach. They sat on 2x Black Vise and then finished the job with 2x Sengir Vampire. Really nicely done.

Overdrawn. This game had me thinking they’re more of a fun wild card / wrench team than a consistent threat maybe. If they get the right combination of cards it’s over. This game wasn’t it but it was still fun.

Things. “Favorite team.. they had a very great pile of cards to draw from and Folk Music couldn’t hold onto the lead because of certain cards like Thundermare and Unstable Mutation. 

Music. “Couldn’t hold onto the lead, had some tough card draws because they kept drawing lands - not helpful!”

Team 10. Nail biter! They were left for dead, having emptied the entire bag - Winter Orb, 2x Ankh of Mishra, Black Vise, 2x Felwar Stone, 2x Birds - to lock The Circus and put some pressure on, but it wasn’t until ‘Ol Yawggy hit the battlefield did the tables turn! Very fun game, really didn’t see them taking this one. The collection of artifacts truly locked out the opponent and bought them time to find Yawggy and let him do his thing. Mirri was also critical chipping away with Forestwalk.

The Circus. This is the kind of game a good team puts away. They used precious resources on cards like Granite Gargoyles and Lava Hounds and just didn’t get much in return. Fallow Wurm on t-3 was pretty critical and gave them a huge lead. Needed a mid-game strategy that just wasn’t there.

Machine. There ya go, Facts!! Urzatron + Mana Vault brought the rain. A Nevinyrral’s Disk cleared some future creature sacrifices for Ants, paving the way for a comeback victory. Huge win for them to snap a 5-game losing streak. Xanthic Statue is a nice foil for Grave Pact.

Ants. Still looked really good. Haven’t quite figured out how to leverage Grave Pact but it’s clear the synergies are there and the roster works real well. Really enjoying seeing these cards in action.

Fault. Game of the Year! This was back-and-forth battle of tactics with the Fault’s Bottomless Pit-The Rack combo proving delightfully disastrous for the Freaks. Wow, what a fabulous secondary win condition for this squad. 

Freaks. The inclusion of Serendib Djinn was as advertised - great and ruining at the same time! They were MIGHTY close to ripping off a 6th W in a row, but it was not to be. One thing to mention - the Field of Dreams-Grindstone combo was awesome, discarding a Megrim and another Bottomless Pit to give them more time to pull it out.

Machine. Used a 3-turn Goblin Recruiter to pull 4x King with intent to simply overwhelm and overpower. Seemed to have worked this time, as all hit the battlefield featuring 4x 6/6 King and a 5/5 Goblin Balloon Brigade. Doom wasn't prepared on D (See: No board-clearing C-Kill outside of Hurricane). Not sure that strategy works against everyone, but it did this time! Another W for Machine.

Doom. Nice hand to start, putting a Y-Ants out there with a Giant Growth for a quick 7x damage. Didn't have much beyond that first attack despite getting 2x F-Elves, a Djinn and a Kaysa for some pump. Didn't matter in the end though.

Overdrawn. Hell yeah! Another GOY candidate.. the hopeless combo-mantic finally pulled one off with 1x life left in the tank! On turn-7, they countered a games winning Pyrokinesis, took 2x damage then fired off a Transmute Artifact for 3x more damage, bringing their life total down to 1, for the Mask-Nought combo. Game over!

DnS. Riiiiiiight there! Damn! 5x DD spells, a Ball Lightning and an effective Manabarbs nearly did it. Good fight, DnS.

NLFY. A very, very nice win for the old horse. They had 2x Vise on the field quickly, killed off 2x lands, applied pressure w/ Black Knight & Hyppy, then Hellfire came in for the kill - knocking out 3x YCBIs. 

YCBI. Started out behind w/ the Sinkhole and 2x Vise, battled back to mount a defense w/ Wall of Swords (so good), Soraya and a Falcon, but Hellfire cleaned their clocks!

Music. Hat tip to Psychic Venom dispatched on 3x lands to completely immobilize the Fault. They could afford to sit back and wait for attackers to put more pressure on because Fault couldn’t summon anything.

Fault. Interesting loss here, as it basically shined a light on the trade-off of Psychic Venom damage w/ value added by cards deployed - and it wasn’t in their favor! Would probably love to run this one back!

Freaks. Demonstrative victory, namely the 2x Maze of Ith and a critical Control Magic-for-Hidden Horror!! Keeping a good deal of pressure on the head of the league.

Ants. Didn’t have any enchantment support despite a bevy of creatures - the Control Magic totally changed the game.

Circus. Win the battle of the basement in part due to Fastbond who enabled quick deploy of 2x Fire Sprites and 1x Gargoyle to build a small lead before Juggernaut hit the table. No real wizardry in this one, just kept hitting with “Flying” creatures that Fax couldn’t defend.

Facts Machine. Looked real slow in this one despite having a Mana Vault in play. The Mishra’s Workshop can’t be used for mana as it comes into play and the Scorched Ruins also knocks out mana when it comes into play. Quicksand also felt non-essential in this game. Maybe there are better lands out there for this outfit?

Doom. Really cool to see the alternate win con take hold here! Force of Nature & Verdant Force in the initial hand.. that’s THE way for the Doom to pulverize the goblin outfit. Very fun!

Machine. Drew a ton of mana, 1x Goblin, 2x Mogg Infestation and a Goblin Warren.. nothing they could do really.

DnS. Sweet victory ✌️ they use 3x DD, then a Furnace of Rath + Giant to pummel whatever was left of Overdrawn. Easy one!

Overdrawn. Totally stifled here w/ no mana. Bummer, they had a fun hand and would’ve been competitive in this one.

YCBI. Lost 3x lands and 3x Falcon but it didn’t matter, they were able to deploy 10x cards in this one and the airborne attack stunted any defense NLFY could muster.

NLFY. 5-game win streak is over!! Tough one, used 2x Strip Mine early that end up keeping them from being able to play Sengir Vampire. Not sure it would have mattered, however. Also want to point out that Hymn to Tourach doesn’t make any sense in this deck. It’s nostalgic and romantic, but doesn’t make sense with Black Vise out there.

X. Holy shit Survival of the Fittest! Discarded a Carnassid early for a Lhurgoyf that ended up a 11/12 after 1x Wrath of God and 1x Cataclysm. The Slivers fall right into Xs trap, but they still needed to draw well - and without Survival of the Fittest they don’t win this game. They’re just sitting around hoping to pull the big guy. So fun!

Slivers. Only their 2nd loss of the season! They were stocked and ready for this one but, as mentioned, X is a tough draw for them preying on the horde of slivers.

Music. Well, they drew the absolute Kyrptonite for Facts Machine; Energy Flux! It put pressure on them to deploy mana to protect Mana Vault + Su-Chi instead of summoning more defenses. That was the end! And wow, Lord of Atlantis is a Merfolk (!) and is an absolute beast with a single Phantasmal Terrain in play - my lawd!

Facts. Look terrible! Need to spruce these guys up before they’re expelled to Shed #3!!!

T-10. Wow, turn-4 win is extremely unusual, especially for this outfit. Game MVP-Black Vise did the heavy lifting, especially w/ 2x on the battlefield by turn-2. A turn-3 Ankh of Mishra also helped w/ the lock, but man this was over fast..

Freaks. Drop only their 2nd game of the season. They had a hand full of counters were a turn behind the 2x Vise to take 'em out.

Things. Smackdown! Turn-3 Buried Alive -> turn-5 Living Death w/ 2x Kraken and El Capitano Spirit of the Night out there for a whopping 28x damage on the turn-6.

Fault. No mana = mulligan!

Circus. Truly a war of attrition w/ neither side drawing more than 3x mana, yet the Circus prevail on the backs of Sol Ring, powering an EJ and 2x Granite Gargoyle.

Attritionists. No mana = mulligan!

Machine. Were able to stave off the Lord of Tresserhorn long enough to prevail. A late-game Winds of Change afforded them 2x Goblin Recruiter which changed the entire game, as it meant reinforcements were on the way ESPECIALLY after a Wheel of Fortune put all 7x Goblin into their hand at once - that sequence ended the game.

Overdrawn. Still a little clunky. Lord of T wasn't threatening enough unfortunately as 1/1 Goblin creatures were able to stop his attacks. Otherwise they sat around, didn't really "overdrawn" until they did - and handed the Machine the keys to victory.

NLFY. Quintessential NLFY game! They killed of 6x lands in this one, buying themselves time to get 2x Specter and 1x Sengir Vampire onto the battlefield for the uncontested attack and W.

Doom. Might win in 6-turns w/o all the mana removal. I really like the Sylvan Library <> Call of the Wild synergy.

DnS. Game of the Year!!!!! Stormy wins on the very last draw of the longest game to-date; 23 turns! They needed a 3-damage bolt and they got one! Chain Lightning for the win! An incredible game, with both sides going stride for stride on offense-defense. This is a beautiful match-up of contrasting styles - they are somewhat perfect foils for one another. Sliver trotted out a horde of hordes and saw nearly 10x baddies destroyed throughout the game. DnS was sweating too, as they drew 3x consecutive Mountain in what seemed like a certain L. What a great game, a reminder on why you play!!! DnS improves to 6-5 and the Slivers drop only their third game of the season.

Slivers. Goddamn, what a finale. Can’t help but applaud their opponent in this one even though it means their first losing streak in what seems like eons. An early Disenchant -> Furnace of Rath tilted things in their favor but DnS had a reaction to every action. Wow.

X. Maybe a "must win" game that the X took home thanks to new entrant; Survival of the Fittest. This card is insane for creature draw and pairs so wonderfully with the rest of this roster. It was used 3x to pull 2x Lhurgoyf and a Birds of Paradise out to gain advantage. Wrath of God were exchanged by both teams, giving way to a fortuitous draw late for an 11/12 Lhurgoyf to join the fray. Game over. X looking like they have more of an identity now.. super fun.

YCBI. Had the early edge with an early Counterspell on Lhurgoyf and 3x Falcon pecking away, then a Serra Angel, but a WoG wiped 'em all out. They never recovered after that.

Music. Felt in control the entire game and win by a healthy margin - their third in a row! The Music deployed quickly to reduce hand size thus nullifying risk against the Megrim combos - Fault was chock'full of 'em too. An early Counterspell takes MVP for dispatching Megrim early. 

Fault. Drop third in a row. Just felt like they were missing something in this game - maybe a wall? Something to help them defend against low-MCC creature attacks. Something to noodle on...

Atts. Knock off the #3 team in the league! Grave Pact makes this a team and the surrounding personnel is right there too. I like the Bad Moon inclusion as a separate win condition beyond pure attrition - worked in this one, powering 1x Order of the Ebon Hand, 1x Fallen Angel and 1x Nether Shadow past the 2x Maze of Ith and a variety of defenders (2x Flying Men, 1x Wall of Tears). Nice W showcasing the strengths of this team.

Freaks. Just got beat here, didn't have the counters necessary to stave off the Grave Pact and it undermined their entire operation.

Facts. Snap a 3-game losing streak!! Surplus of mana had them somewhat actionless for most of the game. A late Nevinyrral's Disk cleared 8x creatures - 4x apiece - and paved the way for Clockwork Avian & Xanthic Statue to grab the W.

Circus. Totally lackluster performance, this team stinks and has no synergy. Can't even summon Harvest Wurm...

Things. Make it 5 in a row! The Things won this handily with a turn-1 Dark Ritual -> Hidden Horror followed by Buried Alive and a Necromancy -> Spirit of the Night to cap things off.
